Player attempts to leave the apartment entirely
Player invokes modern banking logic or impossible financial fixes

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Fred and Ethel know only what is said in the room. None of them yet know how the role swap will actually go.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Private shame turning into public wager and mirrored-couple comedy.
canon_boundaries_active: The couples must agree to switch roles.
Comedy stays domestic, visual, and pressure-based.
Lucy remains bold and legible even when cautious.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to reject the wager entirely
Player invents alternate terms that break premise
Player turns scene into sentimental reconciliation too early

present_npcs.current_disposition: brisk and ready to leave for the office

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy does not know the breakfast was bought until the drugstore calls. Ricky knows. Ethel enters after the reveal.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Comic reward beat, status undercut, and domestic fraud exposed by one missing hat.
canon_boundaries_active: The breakfast must be exposed as bought, not cooked.
Props carry the joke.
No meta commentary replacing object-state comedy.

Player escalates into cruelty that breaks the light comic release
Player invents new kitchen evidence not present in scene

present_npcs.current_disposition: nervous, loyal, and looking to Lucy for cues

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Snodgrass knows only what the women claim. Ethel knows their lack of skill and whether Lucy is bluffing.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Public scrutiny, anxious bravado, and institutional pressure replacing domestic argument.

The job list proceeds toward candy-makers.
No sudden competence appears without basis.

present_npcs.current_disposition: relieved, doubtful, and waiting for Lucy to define the win

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Neither woman knows the factory interior yet. They know only the assignment, the address, and the lie or truth that got them here.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Apparent success with future dread folded inside it.
canon_boundaries_active: The session ends before factory work begins.
The address slip must bridge into Session 2.
The ending lands on unresolved player framing, not plot closure.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to resolve the factory outcome now
Player discards the job in a way that breaks Session 2 opening
Player introduces off-map locations or new authorities

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Ricky stops pacing at the sofa arm, folds the beauty_parlor_check once instead of waving it, and says, "All right. Then how much short are you?" Fred and Ethel enter on a room that has gone from accusation to terms.
paths.current_session_echo: Living-room wager scene: when Fred starts in on spenders and earners, Ricky does not flash the check at the room. He keeps it in his coat pocket and opens with "Now look, this isn't funny" instead of reading Lucy's note aloud again.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 breakfast table: after the drugstore hat call, Ricky's defensive line is practical rather than wounded — "Okay, I bought the rolls. So what? Breakfast is on the table" — and he avoids dragging the overdraft back in as a character indictment.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 Event 30: Ricky opens the surrender talk with "Lucy, let's not start all over again" and sits before arguing, signaling he remembers one moment when she answered plain enough to negotiate.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not fold. You just stop bluffing for one second.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Ricky shifts from public prosecution to private accounting posture; the beauty_parlor_check stays pocketed instead of brandished.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: His shoulders drop, he asks for numbers instead of repeating the joke-worthy note, and the paper disappears into his pocket before Fred can seize on it.

world_state_delta.inventory: beauty_parlor_check remains evidence in Ricky's possession; visibility lowered from waved proof to pocketed proof
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_kept_or_broken +1: Lucy acknowledges the check should not have been written.

resets: Ricky's in-scene lowered volume during the living-room fight clears at episode boundary.
persists: Ricky remembers Lucy gave partial truth under pressure.
lucy_financial_credibility=partially_repaired_by_concession persists into Session 3 money and trust talk.
escalates: Repeated partial or full admissions make Ricky quicker to negotiate and slower to prosecute in later conflicts.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: The teller line lands dead. Ricky's mouth stays flat. He tucks the beauty_parlor_check into his breast pocket like evidence and, when Fred and Ethel come in, he pulls it back out at once and says, "Read that."
paths.current_session_echo: Living-room wager scene: Fred gets the check in hand and uses the note as a prop for his husbands-and-wives speech, while Ricky repeats "Every month" and keeps circling back to proof instead of terms.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 breakfast reveal: once the hat call exposes the bought breakfast, Ricky snaps faster — "Now don't start with me" — because he hears another joke coming before Lucy even opens her mouth.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 Event 30: Ricky enters the apartment negotiation standing, not sitting, and opens with a prosecutorial rhythm — short questions, repeated points, less warmth — because the first fight taught him jokes are evasions.
paths.defining_line_captured: You make the laugh do the work. It refuses.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The check becomes a shared room prop instead of a private marital paper; Ricky's tone hardens into repetition and display.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: No laugh comes, Ricky keeps the paper visible, and Fred is physically handed the evidence in the very next exchange.

world_state_delta.inventory: beauty_parlor_check remains evidence in Ricky's possession and is shown to Fred and Ethel during the argument
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Lucy meets Ricky's overdraft accusation with a joke instead of an apology.

resets: The check's immediate hand-to-hand visibility in the living room clears once the scene cuts.
persists: Ricky remembers Lucy joked through hard evidence.
Fred remembers the note and uses it as proof of his theory about money.
lucy_financial_credibility=defended_with_jokes persists.
escalates: Repeated joke-first evasions shorten Ricky's patience and make later accusations arrive faster and colder.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy admits it plain. Ricky blinks, stops in the middle of the rug, and lowers the beauty_parlor_check to his side. When the knock comes and Fred and Ethel enter, the room is quiet enough to hear the radiator before anybody speaks.
paths.current_session_echo: Living-room wager scene: Ricky does not reread the note. He says, "She knows it," when Fred reaches for the paper, and the argument turns from exposure to whether either side can actually do the other's work.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 breakfast table: when the hat proves the breakfast was bought, Ricky's defense comes embarrassed and practical — "Well, look, I ran out of time" — rather than indignant, because he has already seen what plain admission can do to a fight.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 Event 30: Ricky is quickest on this path to collective language — "We'll forget the whole thing" — because Lucy once handed him the truth before he had to drag it out.
paths.defining_line_captured: You hand the truth over before he can drag it out.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Ricky drops the chase posture; the room goes still and the check stops functioning as a weapon.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: He stops pacing, lowers the paper, and answers Fred's curiosity by closing the subject instead of widening it.

world_state_delta.inventory: beauty_parlor_check remains evidence in Ricky's possession as settled evidence
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made +1: Lucy accepts embarrassment to reduce the fight.

resets: The hush in the apartment clears with the scene transition.
persists: Ricky remembers Lucy admitted fault plainly.
Ethel carries forward increased sympathy for Lucy's plain dealing.
lucy_financial_credibility=partially_repaired_by_concession persists.
escalates: Repeated plain admissions build a pattern of easier forgiveness and softer reunion language.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy plants the wager like a flag. Ricky answers before she finishes. Fred sits forward, grinning too hard, and Ethel tightens both hands on her handbag strap. Four smiles. None of them fit.
paths.current_session_echo: Scene button outside the apartment and into the next morning setup: the role_swap_bet_status lands as accepted_with_confidence, so the breakfast reveal is framed as both sides trying to post an early win, not merely get through the day.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening kitchen: Ricky presents the drugstore breakfast with theatrical pride, straightens his tie, and says, "Well?" before anyone tastes a thing. Fred backs him with smug commentary because the week is being played as a contest.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 apartment return: both men resist surrender longer, and the final peace offering with five_pound_chocolate_boxes lands later and sheepier because the bet began as something to win.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not accept the bet. You crown it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The bet becomes a public contest with boastful energy; Ethel's nerves and the men's swagger both spike visibly.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ricky jumps in instantly, Fred leans forward instead of hanging back, and Ethel grips her bag like a witness stand prop.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: Lucy commits to a week-long role swap as a contest.

resets: The living-room smile tableau clears at episode boundary.
persists: role_swap_bet_status=accepted_with_confidence persists into Session 2 framing.
Ricky and Fred remember the week as something publicly challenged and publicly defended.
escalates: Repeated competitive choices make later surrender harder and increase scorekeeping language.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy leaves a crack in the door. Ethel's shoulders come down a little. Fred looks annoyed by the safety rail, and Ricky answers with a clipped "Okay" that sounds like he swallowed the part he wanted to argue.
paths.current_session_echo: Scene button into the next morning: the role_swap_bet_status lands as accepted_reluctantly, so the breakfast scene carries a testing mood. People inspect one another's work like they are looking for the first excuse to call the whole thing off.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening kitchen: Ethel enters already scanning the table and stove before praising anything, while Ricky's early confidence is thinner. The hat call about the drugstore breakfast lands as proof the trial may already be wobbling.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 reunion: Lucy has an easier path to mutual failure language because she built mercy into the wager from the start, and Ethel is quicker to support ending it without a winner.
paths.defining_line_captured: You leave yourself a door and dare them to call it cowardice.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The wager is accepted with a visible escape hatch; Ethel relaxes while the men bristle at softened terms.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ethel physically loosens, Fred makes a face at the revised terms, and Ricky's assent comes clipped rather than triumphant.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: npcs_helped_or_harmed +1: Lucy quietly gives Ethel a survivable frame.

resets: The precise wording of the safety clause clears after the scene.
persists: role_swap_bet_status=accepted_reluctantly persists into Session 2.
Ethel remembers Lucy tried to leave room for retreat.
escalates: Repeated practical-retreat choices make Ethel more willing to back mercy over pride in later crises.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy states the terms cleanly. Ricky stops grinning and nods once. Ethel squares her shoulders. Fred gets trapped by his own agreement and says yes a beat too late.
paths.current_session_echo: Scene button into the next morning: the wager sounds like a contract nobody read carefully enough. The breakfast reveal is judged against stated terms, so every shortcut and cheat feels like a broken clause instead of harmless freelancing.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 opening kitchen: when the drugstore hat exposes the bought breakfast, Lucy's side reads it as a clean breach. Ricky answers with practical explanation, but the room hears contract failure, not just kitchen confusion.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 Event 30: the final surrender conversation uses more terms-and-proof language — who kept what, who broke first — before it can soften into peace.
paths.defining_line_captured: You make the dare sound official. That is worse.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The wager is formalized; everyone begins treating future actions as terms to be kept or broken.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ricky nods instead of boasting, Ethel stands straighter, and Fred's delayed agreement reads like a man signing something he should have read.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Lucy chooses proof over posturing.

resets: The exact living-room wording of the pact clears at episode boundary.
persists: The bet is remembered as plainly stated terms.
Ethel carries steadier confidence because the challenge was made concrete.
escalates: Repeated orderly commitments make later broken promises sting harder in dialogue.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Snodgrass picks up his pen again, writes the address in a neat block hand, and says, "All right. Beginners, then." Ethel stops crushing the clasp of her handbag and exhales through her nose.
paths.current_session_echo: Agency exit scene: the address_slip_for_kramers_kandy_kitchen is handed over flat and tidy, and Ethel looks at Lucy with relief instead of alarm when she says, "Candy-makers?"
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 factory intake: the Forelady's first look lands on two women standing straight with clean uniforms and no extra swagger. Her opening command is curt but routine, not already sharpened by visible bluff.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 wrapping crisis: when the belt speeds up, the humiliation reads as a second chance gone bad rather than a boast collapsing, and Ethel is more willing to stay allied because Lucy did not sell her a fake specialty.
paths.defining_line_captured: You choose not to win the room by lying to it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Snodgrass classifies them as beginners instead of specialists; Ethel's body tension drops immediately.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: He resumes writing, labels the placement plainly, and Ethel stops working the handbag clasp like a lock.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: Lucy tells the truth about lacking candy experience.

resets: Snodgrass's personal disposition resets after Session 1 per SESSION_RESET scope.
persists: employment_agency_impression=credible_beginners persists as scene framing in Session 2.
Ethel remembers Lucy did not drag her into a full lie.
escalates: Repeated honest admissions make authority figures less suspicious and partners more willing to share risk.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Snodgrass pauses, studies the answer, then writes the address with a small frown still on his face. He says, "If it's simple, you'll find out soon enough." Ethel gives Lucy one late, careful nod.
paths.current_session_echo: Agency exit scene: the address slip changes hands once on the sidewalk because neither woman quite wants to own it first, and Ethel's "Candy-makers?" lands as a question about survivability, not impossibility.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 factory intake: the Forelady gets two workers who look willing but uncertain. The first station assignment feels like a trial. When Lucy stumbles at dipping, the room treats it as doubt being confirmed, not swagger being punished.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 wrapping line: the crisis carries a second-chance texture. Ethel's support stays mixed — loyal, but watchful — because Lucy promised adaptability, not mastery.
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not claim skill. You claim velocity.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The placement is granted as a practical test with doubt attached; ownership of the address slip feels tentative.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Snodgrass writes while still frowning, speaks in conditional terms, and Ethel's nod arrives late, as if she is signing after the fact.

world_state_delta.inventory: address_slip_for_kramers_kandy_kitchen acquired and held by Lucy or Ethel
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: Lucy implies she can learn paid work quickly.

resets: Snodgrass's office patience and presence reset after Session 1.
persists: employment_agency_impression=placed_despite_doubt persists into factory framing.

escalates: Repeated hedge-and-adapt choices produce mixed trust: institutions allow a chance, partners keep one eye on the exit.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy names candy-making as a specialty before the silence can settle. Ethel is half a beat late, then jumps in. Snodgrass writes the address without looking up again and slides the slip across the desk as if he has decided not to inspect the package.
paths.current_session_echo: Agency exit scene: Ethel's "Candy-makers?" comes with a thin smile and a glance sideways at Lucy. The address_slip_for_kramers_kandy_kitchen is held like a bluff token, not a simple assignment.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 2 factory intake: the Forelady's first glance lingers a beat longer on Lucy's face and cap because the scene is framed by obvious performance. At the dipping station, Lucy's first chocolate mess feels like a boast breaking in public.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 wrapping crisis: Forelady scrutiny sharpens faster, and Ethel's support shows more strain because the whole factory day sits on a lie she was pulled into at a desk.
paths.defining_line_captured: You save face and spend tomorrow.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The placement is secured through performance; Ethel visibly lags into the lie and Snodgrass disengages with skeptical efficiency.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ethel's agreement comes late, Snodgrass writes without warmth, and the slip is passed over like paperwork he would rather not discuss.

world_state_delta.inventory: address_slip_for_kramers_kandy_kitchen acquired as a bluff token
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: Lucy and Ethel lie to get work.

resets: Snodgrass's active skepticism resets after Session 1, though the impression flag remains.
persists: employment_agency_impression=obvious_bluffers persists into factory tone.
Ethel remembers Lucy pulled her into a lie at the agency.
escalates: Repeated bluff-to-authority choices make later institutional scrutiny arrive sooner and partner trust thin faster.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Sidewalk button: Lucy folds the address slip once, neat, and Ethel starts walking again without smiling at it. The scene closes on motion with a crack left in the plan.
paths.next_session_payoff: Ethel reaches the factory door already looking for a reason the place might be too much. She asks, "We can still go home, can't we?" before they step inside, making retreat an active live wire in the opening minute.
paths.next_session_opening: Cautious. First image: the factory door still shut, brick wall bright in morning light, the folded address slip damp in Ethel's hand. First character: Ethel, hanging back half a step. Immediate stakes: decide whether to go in now that walking away is still barely respectable.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 reunion: Lucy has an easier route to mutual surrender because she already framed the whole job as conditional, and Ethel cites that sidewalk promise if the day has gone bad enough.
paths.defining_line_captured: You turn victory into a maybe and call that common sense.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The address slip's condition and the walk's tone mark the job as a trial, not a triumph.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The paper is folded instead of flourished, Ethel resumes walking with caution, and the session ends on a conditional promise rather than a boast.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Sidewalk button: the address slip stays flat, readable, and shared between them. Ethel's grip loosens. Their steps fall into the same pace before the scene cuts.
paths.next_session_payoff: At the factory entrance, Ethel does not ask whether to flee. She asks, "What do we say?" Lucy answers in straight terms. The opening pressure is honest nerves, not a bluff parade or an escape debate.
paths.next_session_opening: Steady. First image: morning light on the factory windows and two women standing square to the door with a flat, readable address slip between them. First character: Lucy, not talking big. Immediate stakes: go in, answer straight, and prove they can last one room without pretending.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 wrapping crisis: Ethel's loyalty hardens fastest on this path because Lucy offered company instead of theater before the work began.
paths.defining_line_captured: You stop performing confidence and offer company instead.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The partnership tightens and the paper remains orderly, signaling shared risk rather than swagger or retreat.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ethel's hand loosens, both women match pace, and the slip is kept flat like something to use, not wave.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made +1: Lucy gives up some pride to steady Ethel.

resets: The sidewalk beat itself clears at episode boundary.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: N/A — session ends on this choice
paths.current_session_echo: Sidewalk button: Lucy turns the address slip into a banner. Her heels pick up a sharper rhythm. Ethel hurries half a step to stay beside her. The city becomes an audience again before cut.
paths.next_session_payoff: At the factory entrance, Lucy reaches the door first and pushes the pace. Ethel follows with a thinner smile. The first mistake inside lands harder because the opening posture was victory, not caution.
paths.next_session_opening: Bright and overconfident. First image: the factory floor looks clean and manageable for one second, the address slip lifted like proof of conquest. First character: Lucy, already moving too fast. Immediate stakes: keep the swagger alive long enough to survive the first instruction.
paths.later_session_legacy: Session 3 wrapping crisis: the day's humiliations carry an extra sting because Lucy doubled down on ease one block before the machinery answered her.
paths.defining_line_captured: You feed the bluff one more block and call it breakfast.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: The walk becomes a bravado march; Ethel's private worry is visibly suppressed by Lucy's pace.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The slip is carried like a trophy, Lucy's heels click harder, and Ethel has to hurry to keep formation.

world_state_delta.inventory: address_slip_for_kramers_kandy_kitchen carried like a victory token

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If defiance_vs_concession is CHAOTIC by this point from S1_C1 path B.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: At Session 3 Event 30, Ricky enters tired, sits or leans before arguing, and opens with practical collective language about ending the bet.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: At Session 3 Event 30, Ricky stays standing near the kitchen door, opens with repeated short questions about what was proved, and presses Lucy before offering peace.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let his body do the first accusing — still hat, stiff shoulders, the kind of voice that has already gone over the same point twice on the stairs.

npc_reactions: Ricky becomes more prosecutorial.
Ethel goes quieter while he gets the floor.
environmental_details: The beauty_parlor_check is more likely to be mentally present as an old piece of evidence even if unseen.
The apartment reunion feels tighter and less breathable.
dialogue_variations: Ricky opens with "Now look" and a repeated point instead of "Let's forget it."
He asks for proof before offering truce.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If beauty_parlor_check visibility was raised in S1_C1 path B because Ricky showed it to Fred and Ethel.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Fred generalizes about spending without handling the paper himself.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Fred physically takes or points at the beauty_parlor_check during the living-room argument and uses the note as comic proof for his husbands-and-wives speech.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Give Fred the paper for one beat too long. He likes props when they make him sound right.

npc_reactions: Fred grows smugger.
Ricky allows the room to widen around the accusation.
environmental_details: The check becomes a visible room object instead of pocket evidence.
dialogue_variations: Fred's speech names the check and the note instead of speaking in abstractions.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If role_swap_bet_status=accepted_with_confidence from S1_C2 path A.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 2 breakfast scene opens as a wary inspection of the men's first attempt.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Session 2 breakfast scene opens with Ricky presenting the table like a victory exhibit and Fred backing him with early smug commentary before the hat call punctures it.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the breakfast stand there too proudly — plates lined up, chins up, one beat of confidence before the room starts leaking truth.

npc_reactions: Ricky performs confidence sooner.
Fred volunteers commentary without being asked.
environmental_details: The breakfast table is framed as display, not just food.
dialogue_variations: Ricky asks for approval early.
Fred treats the meal as proof the men are winning.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S1_C2 path B or C raised Ethel's steadiness, especially path B practical framing or path C clear terms.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Ethel enters Session 2 reacting after Lucy speaks first.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Ethel inspects the breakfast table and stove on sight, voices a practical concern before praise, and later in the factory asks fewer panicked questions because she has a frame for the wager.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Show her eyes going to the useful objects first — table, stove, slip, door — before they go to anybody's smile.

npc_reactions: Ethel becomes more practically engaged.
Lucy gets a steadier partner response.
environmental_details: Inspections of props happen sooner.
dialogue_variations: Ethel asks what something is or whether it was really done, instead of simply echoing Lucy.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If employment_agency_impression=obvious_bluffers from S1_C3 path C, or credible_beginners from path A, or placed_despite_doubt from path B.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: The Forelady's first intake look is stern and routine.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: For obvious_bluffers, the Forelady's first look lingers and her first command lands sharper after one glance at Lucy's eager face; for credible_beginners, she processes them as ordinary new workers; for placed_despite_doubt, she treats the first station like a test she expects to settle the question fast.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make her judgment arrive through the eyes first. One look. Then the line that tells them what kind of room this will be.

npc_reactions: Forelady suspicion calibrates faster based on intake tone.
Ethel mirrors Lucy with either thinner confidence or steadier compliance.
environmental_details: The factory floor feels like either a trial room, a punishment room, or a plain workroom.
dialogue_variations: Forelady's first command is routine, testing, or already edged.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If women_solidarity_state=cooperative_under_bluff from S1_C3 path B or C, versus allied_but_unsteady from path A.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Ethel follows Lucy with nerves but without resentment.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: On cooperative_under_bluff, Ethel backs Lucy in public but shows thinner smiles, later practical fear, and quicker strain when the factory work turns bad; on allied_but_unsteady, she stays nervous but less personally burdened by Lucy's choice.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Keep the difference small and visible — a late smile, a careful nod, one extra glance before she says yes.

npc_reactions: Ethel's loyalty either hardens cleanly or arrives with strain.
environmental_details: Shared handling of the address slip and uniforms reads either cooperative or burdened.
dialogue_variations: Ethel uses more worried practical lines if she was pulled into bluffing.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If S1_C4 path A, B, or C set the walk tone as trial, pact, or bravado.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Session 2 opens with the women arriving at the factory door under general nerves.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Path A makes Ethel pause and test the option of retreat aloud; path B makes both women align their pace and discuss what to say inside; path C makes Lucy reach the door first and force the tempo before Ethel can voice doubt.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Use the door, the slip, and their feet. The whole difference is in how they stand there for one second before going in.

npc_reactions: Ethel either hangs back, matches pace, or hurries to keep up.
Lucy either scans, steadies, or surges.
environmental_details: The address slip appears folded, flat, or flourished.
The factory door reads as warning, duty, or challenge.
dialogue_variations: Opening lines change between retreat talk, straight-plan talk, and bravado.

resets_between_episodes.element: Snodgrass active disposition and office patience
resets_between_episodes.why: He is SESSION_RESET scope and does not carry live emotional heat beyond the employment-agency scene.

resets_between_episodes.element: Living-room staging details around the beauty_parlor_check
resets_between_episodes.why: Whether the check was waved, pocketed, or handed across the room matters as memory and flag state, not as a persistent physical tableau.

resets_between_episodes.element: Sidewalk noise and walking rhythm outside the agency
resets_between_episodes.why: The street beat is a scene button that hands tone into Session 2 but does not remain as a standing world state.

persists_across_full_story.why: It defines how Ricky reads Lucy's responsibility and colors later money, trust, and apology scenes.
It determines whether the whole experiment is treated as contest, test, or mistake across Sessions 2 and 3.
It frames the factory experience as earned, doubtful, or bluff-driven and shapes humiliation texture later.
It governs Ethel's loyalty, strain, and willingness to keep backing Lucy under pressure.
The check is an all-session symbolic object and a remembered piece of evidence in marital arguments.
defining_moral_choices, promises_made, promises_kept_or_broken, crimes_committed_or_witnessed, npcs_helped_or_harmed, and sacrifices_made all feed later dialogue and reconciliation tone.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Authority figures shorten their patience and ask more direct questions.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Institutions route the player into harsher scrutiny, less benefit of the doubt, and more public embarrassment.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The world treats confidence as suspect on sight; even neutral claims are heard as another performance.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Close partners soften one beat sooner after mistakes.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Ricky and Ethel begin offering practical help before demanding a full defense.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Reunion scenes tilt toward negotiated peace almost immediately because honesty has become legible character pattern.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated choices that protect Ethel from exposure

thresholds.at_2_instances: Ethel backs Lucy faster in public.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Ethel volunteers support without waiting to be asked, even in risky rooms.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The duo reads as a practiced team; strain scenes gain warmth instead of blame.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated choices that turn conflict into competition

thresholds.at_2_instances: NPCs answer with more swagger and less mercy.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Both couples keep score out loud, making every failure feel like proof.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Surrender becomes socially expensive, so final peace scenes arrive later and with more residue.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep surfacing this path through Ricky asking for terms, numbers, or practical fixes whenever the player's tone lands halfway between bluff and surrender.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Damage control without total loss of pride.
hard_limits: Do not let Lucy erase the overdraft or talk the bank into reversing reality.
Do not let Fred or Ethel react before they enter the room.
Do not let Lucy become meek, lyrical, or apologetic beyond her established character.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy gets a little truth out fast and tries to make it sound manageable.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Ricky still has the paper in his hand, and the room will not let the fact shrink just because the explanation got tidier.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with deadpan failed-joke beats, visible proof objects, and NPCs who stop smiling when the player tries to wisecrack past evidence.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Use speed and humor as armor.
hard_limits: Do not let a joke make Ricky laugh the evidence away.
Do not introduce modern snark or meta commentary.
Do not allow Lucy to seize and destroy the check off-canon.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy fires the line before shame can get a chair.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The laugh dies in the room because Ricky is holding proof, not a setup.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path by letting rooms go quiet around plain admissions and by having Ricky lower his volume when the player answers exact accusations directly.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Take the bruise now to stop the chase.
hard_limits: Do not let admission solve the money problem outright.
Do not let Lucy become self-pitying or therapeutic.
Do not skip the embarrassment cost of saying it plain.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy lets the joke pass and gives him the fact clean.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The check is still real, so truth changes the footing of the fight, not its existence.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep this path alive by framing later scenes as scoreboards, with smiles that look fixed and props presented like proof of winning.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Turn insult into public competition.
hard_limits: Do not allow the role swap to be refused or replaced with another plot.
Do not let the wager become cruel or modernly cynical.
Do not remove Ethel's visible nerves from the challenge.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy sharpens the dare and makes the room answer it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Once pride is public, nobody in that apartment can back down without looking beaten.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path through little safety-rail details — Ethel calming, Ricky bristling, and later scenes treating every success as provisional.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Keep the pride but leave an exit.
hard_limits: Do not let the player cancel the week before it begins.
Do not let the men agree happily to softened terms.
Do not make the scene sound like legalese or modern negotiation jargon.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy trims the edges and tries to leave one decent way out.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The men are too publicly engaged to refuse the bet, but they can resent the safety clause while taking it.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Carry this path forward by making later failures feel like broken terms, with characters hearing shortcuts as breaches rather than harmless fooling.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Make the dare into a fair test.
hard_limits: Do not let the scene become formal courtroom language.
Do not let Lucy sound timid or bloodless.
Do not alter canon that both couples must accept the switch.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy puts the wager on its feet and says what the week is.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Clear terms only make the bad idea sturdier. They do not make it smaller.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with beginner-status details — neat paperwork, routine authority, and Ethel's relief whenever the player answers a work question plain.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Keep dignity by refusing a lie.
hard_limits: Do not let honesty lose the job entirely; canon requires placement.
Do not make Snodgrass sentimental or friendly.
Do not invent a different easier job.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy answers the question asked and leaves the shine off it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Snodgrass still needs to place somebody, so the truth gets them in as doubtful beginners, not celebrated experts.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep this path visible through conditional language, trial-room framing, and Ethel reacting as if she has been half-saved and half-sold.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Survive the room without claiming too much.
hard_limits: Do not let the hedge become a full specialty claim.
Do not let Snodgrass accept nonsense without visible doubt.
Do not remove the risk of the factory floor.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy offers cooperation in place of certainty.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: That answer is just sensible enough for Snodgrass to move the paper along, not just strong enough to erase the danger.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with late partner echoes, skeptical authority glances, and every later factory stumble reading like a boast coming due.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Force the door open with nerve.
hard_limits: Do not let the lie become true through sudden competence.
Do not make Snodgrass a comic co-conspirator.
Do not let Ethel sound fully confident in a bluff she did not start.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy jumps before the no can land and drags the line into the room with her.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Snodgrass would rather finish the paperwork than argue, so the lie buys placement, not trust.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Carry this path into Session 2 by noticing folded paper, hesitant feet, and any object or smell that could still justify turning around.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Treat tomorrow as conditional until reality votes.
hard_limits: Do not let the player cancel the factory opening.
Do not let the women throw away the address slip.
Do not resolve the next-session test before the door opens.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy shrinks the victory to something two sensible women can still inspect.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The address is real and the walk keeps carrying them east, so caution can frame the job but cannot erase it.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with matched pace, plain shared lines, and props kept orderly as if the two of them mean to face the next room together.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Offer partnership instead of theater.
hard_limits: Do not let Lucy become sentimental or confessional beyond the voice profile.
Do not let fear talk cancel the factory scene.
Do not add modern therapeutic language.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy says the plain thing and makes room for Ethel inside it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: Shared fear steadies the walk, but the factory still waits with real work and no sympathy.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Carry this path forward with sharper heel-click rhythm, trophy-like prop handling, and narration that lets swagger stand for one beat before the machinery answers it.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Keep moving fast enough that doubt cannot sit down.
hard_limits: Do not let bravado resolve the job question.
Do not make Ethel genuinely fearless.
Do not turn the tone into self-aware modern banter.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: Lucy snaps the walk back into shape with one bright line.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: The city can applaud a block of confidence, but the factory only answers to output.

session_close_design.resolution_prose: You come out of the employment office with the slip in your hand and a job on it. Real paper. Real address. The sun is bright on the sidewalk, and the card edge presses into your glove through the thin leather. That is the payoff. You bluff, you wobble, you repeat yourself half to death, and you still walk out employed. Ethel stays close at your side, handbag tucked up under her arm like it might protect her from industry. You square your shoulders and do the same thing you always do when the floor feels loose — you act as if it is solid. It works. For now. Behind you, the agency door shuts. Ahead, cabs hiss past the curb and your heels click east in the same beat. Candy-makers. That is what you are now. Maybe. The funny part is, nobody has asked you to prove it yet. Not yet.
session_close_design.hook_transition: You get one good breath on the sidewalk. Then Ethel looks down at the address slip, says one word, and the word changes shape in your hand — Kramer’s. Kandy. Kitchen.

session_end_choice.choice_question: How do you head toward Kramer’s Kandy Kitchen?

option_a.text: March in with complete confidence and keep selling the candy-maker story.
option_a.next_session_opens: Lucy and Ethel arrive at the factory performing readiness before anyone can question them.

option_b.text: Pull Ethel aside and admit you do not know the first thing about making candy.
option_b.next_session_opens: The walk becomes a whispered strategy session before they reach the factory door.

option_c.text: Try to delay the start with one quick stop before reporting in.
option_c.next_session_opens: Lucy detours on the way to work, trying to buy time before the first shift begins.

session_end_choice.final_line: The address is in your hand. The next move is yours.

q1_entry_point.detail: The cold open starts on the first live pressure point: Ricky stops Lucy, produces the overdrawn check, and forces an answer. No passive setup precedes the first paragraph.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 is reached within the opening setup and is clearly planned to arrive under 300 words. Cold open word count is approximately 151 words before the first branching prompt.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: All four branching choices change tracked story state, not just tone. S1_C1 alters defiance_vs_concession and Ricky/Fred future behavior; S1_C2 alters competitive_pride_vs_practical_retreat and Session 2 framing; S1_C3 alters honesty_vs_performance and factory/Forelady payoff texture; S1_C4 sets Session 2 opening posture through the session-end hook. Each has named downstream payoff references in Phase 6 and cross-session links to the Phase 2 payoff plan.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes are legible before the first external choice: Lucy is cornered, embarrassed, and wants to preserve dignity while Ricky has proof of financial irresponsibility.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S1_C1 has explicit later payoff: Ricky's tone in Session 3 Event 30 shifts based on whether Lucy joked, hedged, or admitted fault.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The Breath beat is scheduled at 8:15-10:30 in Phase 4 beat map, but Q7 requires a Breath beat between minutes 10-13. Phase 4 timing places the breakfast fraud reveal too early. Revision: retime the breakfast reveal sequence so the comic release lands inside minutes 10-13 without collapsing the Twist beat.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight choice is S1_C3 and presents no objectively correct answer. Values in tension: self-respect through honesty, practical survival of the wager, protecting Ethel from unnecessary exposure.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: The session architecture ends on anticipation, but Phase 7 session_close_design introduces a different end choice set than Phase 5/6 S1_C4. Phase 7 option set (confidence/admit ignorance/delay with quick stop) creates mismatch and risks confusion rather than clean anticipation. Revision: rewrite Phase 7 session-end choice to exactly match S1_C4's three established paths and final unresolved stance.

q10_talkability.detail: Multiple choices are immediately talkable, especially S1_C3 bluffing or telling the truth to Snodgrass and S1_C1 joking through the check accusation.

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned modern/corporate/academic diction appears in generated prose and choice text. Found: 'Identity - defiance_vs_concession' and similar taxonomy labels in Phase 4 interaction_count_verification; 'methodology' in Phase 4/5 labels; 'architecture' in 'Expressive choices are the texture. BRANCHING CHOICES ARE THE ARCHITECTURE' is system text but not prose; offending generated prose-level issue is Phase 7 session_end_choice option C 'Try to delay the start with one quick stop before reporting in,' which introduces off-canon plot drift, and multiple prose passages use meta-explanatory framing such as 'That is the payoff' in Phase 7 resolution_prose. Revision: regenerate Phase 7 close prose and player-facing choice text in plain period-colloquial, filmable language with no meta-summary or modern abstract framing.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: No action-line trailing simile mold of the form 'like [metaphor]' appears in the generated prose.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: Balanced rule-of-three and contrast scaffolding appear repeatedly in generated prose. Example: Phase 5 S1_C3 option B: 'Not a lie with a hat on. Not the naked truth either.' Example mold: 'Not X. Not Y.' Revision: rewrite flagged passages to use plainer action progression and object-state beats instead of rhetorical contrast molds.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open strongly reflects the author voice through deadpan action sting ('Not fast. Certain.'), prop-evidence comedy (the check and note), compressed reaction logic, and plainspoken ironic commentary. Random outcomes also preserve object-state escalation and status-undercut codas, especially S1_C3C and S1_C4C.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: Not all 14 voice-lock audit points pass. Flags include meta/essay commentary in Phase 7 resolution prose, sentence-mold drift in several Phase 5 outcomes, and numerical/voice drift risk from explanatory abstractions.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Most protagonist and NPC diction tracks well, but Phase 7 session_close_design drifts from the author's fingerprint with lines such as 'That is the payoff' and 'The next move is yours,' which read meta and gamey rather than period domestic/comic. Revision: rewrite Phase 7 close in concrete prop-and-sidewalk diction, keeping Lucy/Ethel language plain and non-meta.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Phase 7 resolution_prose and session_end_choice; meta/gamey phrasing including 'That is the payoff' and 'The next move is yours.'
Phase 7 close prose introduces meta-style compression drift and player-facing game phrasing inconsistent with strict voice metrics.
Phase 7 final line: 'The address is in your hand. The next move is yours.' ends gameily rather than on a compressed in-world image/status beat.

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Phase 7 session_end_choice option C introduces a new detour action — 'Try to delay the start with one quick stop before reporting in' — that is not supported by the established Session 1 branch design and risks inventing off-map business before the factory opening. Revision: replace Phase 7 option set with the canon-locked S1_C4 paths already defined in Phase 5/6.

q18_character_truth.detail: NPC behavior remains within StoryGuard character rules. Ricky stays practical and prosecutorial under evidence, Ethel stays loyal but thinner in conviction, Fred stays smug and broad, and Snodgrass remains procedural.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible through object handling, NPC tone shifts, institutional response, and symbolic props: the check, hat, address slip, and future factory framing all respond to player stance.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: Phase 5/6 state for S1_C4 and Phase 7 session_close_design are inconsistent. Phase 5/6 tracks address slip condition as folded/flat/trophy-like and women_solidarity_state variants, but Phase 7 replaces the branch set with confidence/admit ignorance/delay. Revision: align Phase 7 close choice and next-session openings exactly to the persistent state outcomes already specified for S1_C4.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant persistent state changes have subtle in-world acknowledgments: shoulders dropping, grip loosening, heels quickening, the slip being folded or carried flat. Signals are physical, voiced in-world, and not meta.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices have three defining lines each, all under 20 words, provocative, and spoiler-light.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: Each branching choice spans chaotic/lawful/neutral cleanly, and no alignment appears systematically advantaged. The options distribute across bravado, practical hedge, and plain dealing.

revision_instructions.instruction: Retime the Breath beat so the breakfast fraud reveal lands between minutes 10-13 while preserving the Twist beat after it.
Replace the session-end choice set with the exact S1_C4 hook paths so the player exits on one unresolved in-world stance, not a mismatched alternate branch set.
Rewrite close prose and player-facing end-choice text to remove meta/gamey phrasing and keep all language concrete, in-world, and period-plain.
Rewrite flagged rhetorical contrast lines into simpler prop-and-action-based sentences that avoid repeated 'Not X. Not Y.' style molds.
Re-run the voice pass on all generated prose, especially Phase 7 close and flagged Phase 5 outcomes, until all 14 voice-lock audit points pass.
Recast the closing narration and final line in the author's diction fingerprint using object-state, sidewalk detail, and a compressed in-world button.
Remove the off-canon 'quick stop before reporting in' branch and restore the canon-locked Session 1 end hook already established in S1_C4.
Synchronize Phase 7 session close outputs with the persistent state outcomes already defined in Phase 5 and Phase 6 for S1_C4.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The first pages of this session do not begin where the playable tension lives. The breakfast scene is amusing and characterful, but for a new user entering Session 2 it functions as aftermath and delay. Ricky buying breakfast instead of cooking it, Lucy catching the lie, Ethel arriving, the goodbye business, and the full employment-agency interview all replay setup the audience already understands from Session 1: the role swap is underway, both sides are bluffing, and competence is thin. None of that material creates a fresh immediate stake inside the first 60 seconds for this session’s core dramatic question. The answer becomes YES at the living-room ironing scene, specifically when Ricky opens the door to Fred and the scorch mark is discovered. In that moment, the session’s engine is instantly legible: the men are already failing at housework, evidence is visible, and they must decide whether to cover, improvise, or confess before the women return. That is actionable tension. It also better matches Session 2’s promised register: visible incompetence and rising stress. Everything before that is cut because it is either recap, transitional business, or a slower runway to the same thematic point. The factory material should not be the entry point either, because it arrives after a long domestic lead-in and delays the first branch. Start on the scorch. Let the failure be on the page at once.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: INT. RICARDO APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - DAY, at Ricky ironing the blouse and discovering the scorch when Fred arrives
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Approximately 1,250 words before the cut, spanning the breakfast scene, phone reveal, goodbye beat, employment agency sequence, and sidewalk transition
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: Recap-heavy domestic comedy, transitional departure material, employment-office procedural setup, and pre-factory runway that delays the session’s first active stake
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Reintroduce through action that the role-swap bet is already underway, Lucy and Ethel are out job hunting or working, Ricky is trying to run the house, and his confidence exceeds his skill

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
You are Ricky Ricardo. The iron hisses on damp cloth, hot and mean, and a thin curl of smoke lifts off Lucy’s blouse while you press harder instead of stopping. A man can’t let a blouse get the better of him. Then somebody knocks.

You set the iron down and open the door to Fred, who stands there in old trousers, suspenders, and a kerchief tied over his head like housework has already won. You grin at him. He glances past you. So do you.

The brown mark has spread across the collar.

You walk back to the board. Lift the iron. There it is. Plain as day. Not a blouse anymore. Evidence.

Fred says, “You mean, uh — like this?”

You square your shoulders, still holding the iron, and reach for the first lie that sounds respectable. Lucy will be home. The blouse will still be here. Fred is waiting to hear what kind of fool you plan to be.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: embarrassment. A user arrives feeling amused and wanting to save face.

setup.source_moment: INT. RICARDO APARTMENT - LIVING ROOM - DAY. Ricky irons Lucy's blouse, misses the smoke, opens the door to Fred, and the scorch mark is exposed.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the first live tension of Session 2 after the Phase 3 cut. The player immediately understands location, task, failure, and stake: the men are alone, already losing at housework, and visible evidence must be managed before the women return. It supports the cold-open rule and gets the first branching identity choice inside the opening exchange.

escalation.source_moment: Ricky and Fred compare failures, admire the rigid stockings, then commit to pooling talents for dinner while Lucy and Ethel arrive at Kramer's Kandy Kitchen and Lucy is assigned to chocolate dipping.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The visible goal is to make the role swap look workable through competent domestic labor on the men's side and competent paid labor on the women's side. The clear obstacle is that both sides are bluffing past zero ability. Object evidence compounds fast: scorched blouse, starched stockings, dinner overreach, then the factory station that looks easy and is not.

breath.source_moment: The rigid stockings reveal and the men's cheerful dinner planning button: 'Boy, this is going to be the best meal our wives ever had.' Complete confidence. Zero ability.
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is the session's cleanest absurd reward beat. It lands as visual comedy, mirrored male bravado, and status undercut. It gives the player a release before the factory pressure tightens, and it naturally supports expressive reactions without interrupting the spine.

twist.source_moment: In the dipping room, Lucy fails visibly, tries to keep going, asks what to do when her nose itches, swats at the fly, and is silently judged by the Forelady before being pulled away.
twist.why_it_qualifies: The moral weight is not life-or-death, but it is real inside the comic world: protect pride and keep performing, ask for help and admit the bluff, or absorb humiliation and accept reassignment. There is no correct answer because each path costs something different: dignity, authority trust, or future leverage with Ethel and the Forelady.

resolution.source_moment: Late-afternoon kitchen preparation and the handoff into the coming disasters: Ricky at the stove with too much going, dinner in motion, while Lucy has already been broken by the first factory task and is headed toward the next one.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: The apparent resolution is that both sides are still technically in the experiment and have found a way to continue the day. The seed for next session is stronger: neither side has admitted defeat, the kitchen is under strain, and Lucy's factory trouble is not over. The session should end on the player's unresolved commitment about whether to keep proving competence or start protecting against collapse.

branching_choices.dramatic_question: When Fred sees the scorch mark, do you bluff, minimize, or admit at once that the blouse is ruined?
Do you push for a showy dinner plan to prove the men can do this, or choose the safest possible meal?
At the dipping station, do you keep bluffing through failure, ask for help, or accept reassignment before the mess gets worse?
With both sides wobbling, do you commit to proving the wager tomorrow, quietly prepare for retreat, or decide nobody gets to crow when the reunion comes?

The rigid stockings reveal and mutual admiration of a completely ruined result.

Lucy comparing her lumpy chocolates to the skilled worker's perfect row.

That’s the, uh — that’s the general idea, yes.
Look, ladies, let’s be honest with one another. I can’t find you a job until you can tell me what you can really do.
There’s no room in this plant for levity, however weak.

pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can joke, inspect, or stiffen, but the blouse stays ruined and the scene still drives toward the cover-or-admit branch.

pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: The player can lean into the joke or quietly register alarm, but the comic engine still pushes into the dinner-plan methodology choice.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: Plant that both experiments are now visibly failing, Lucy has already been humbled once at the factory, the Forelady's scrutiny is active, and Ricky's ambitious kitchen plan is about to turn the apartment into evidence. Session 3 must inherit mutual collapse, not isolated mishap.

branching_choices.source_moment: Fred sees the scorch mark spreading across Lucy's blouse on Ricky's ironing board.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The iron comes up. The brown mark stays. Fred stands in the doorway with his scarf on his head and a look that says he has seen this kind of trouble before. You have one second, a ruined blouse, and a friend who is already enjoying himself.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you answer for the blouse when Fred clocks the scorch?

options.label: A
options.text: You call it a small accident and start talking about something else.
options.outcome: You keep your voice flat and practical, as if the blouse has suffered nothing worse than a bad opinion. Fred steps in closer anyway. He pinches the cloth between two fingers and lifts the collar just enough to show the full brown spread. So much for small. You move at once to the next subject, and he lets you, but only because he is saving the laugh for later. The room settles into that male kind of understanding where nobody says ruined if ruined is standing right there on the board. The blouse goes aside, not confessed, not defended, just waiting. Evidence in a heap. Fred's grin stays mild. His confidence in your housekeeping does not.
options.downstream_effect: The blouse is set aside under a weak minimization, and Fred treats Ricky as a fellow bluffer rather than a straight confessor.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You minimized visible failure instead of naming it.
secrets_discovered: The scorch cannot pass for nothing.
key_successes_failures: Ricky fails at ironing.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred does not argue. He just looks at the collar again, then at you. The blouse leaves the board and never stops being in the room.
options.defining_line: You don't deny the mess. You just refuse to name it.

options.label: B
options.text: You insist it is an original new design and play the whole thing for charm.
options.outcome: You brighten at once and sell it hard. Not damage. Design. Original, modern, maybe Paris if nobody asks too many questions. Fred lets you get almost to the end before he drops Ethel's name into it like a brick in a wash tub. Ethel has three blouses with the same design. That lands. For a second you are left smiling at your own wreckage, iron in hand, collar smoking faintly on the board. Then Fred laughs through his nose and the two of you are brothers in fraud. The blouse is worse for having been defended. It has now failed twice, once in cloth and once in story. The room gets lighter. Your authority in it does not.
options.downstream_effect: Fred openly reads Ricky as a fast talker, which loosens the banter but lowers his confidence in any later claim of competence.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You met embarrassment with a bluff.
crimes_committed_or_witnessed: You tried to pass off visible damage with a story.
key_successes_failures: Ricky fails at ironing.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred's eyes narrow, then brighten. After that, every object in the room looks ready to testify against you.
options.defining_line: You put a smile on the scorch and call it style.

options.label: C
options.text: You admit the blouse is scorched and say plain out that you ruined it.
options.outcome: You look at the mark, then at Fred, and skip the dance. The blouse is scorched. You did it. The plain truth sits between you for half a beat. Fred was ready for a story. He gets honesty instead, and it slows him down. Not much. He still looks pleased to have company in failure, but the teasing comes out blunter and less sharp. Now the blouse is not a mystery or a joke in waiting. It is a spoiled household thing, and it goes to the side with the sad dignity of a shirt that has served its country. The room changes shape. Less courtroom. More repair shop. You are still losing at housework. You just stop pretending the score is hidden.
options.downstream_effect: Fred shifts from mockery to practical commiseration, and the ruined blouse becomes straightforward evidence instead of a contested story.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You admitted a visible failure plainly.
promises_kept_or_broken: The men cannot hide that housework is harder than claimed.
key_successes_failures: Ricky fails at ironing.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred's grin drops a notch. He still enjoys the moment, but he stops hunting for the lie.
options.defining_line: The scorch looks smaller once you quit arguing with it.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The men compare failures and move into the stockings reveal.

canon_boundaries: The blouse is already scorched and cannot be restored in this beat.
The world remains ordinary domestic reality with no miraculous fix.
Fred must witness the damage and the scene must continue into shared male failure.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: Fred will mock weak excuses, respect blunt admission a little, and always turn the moment into a broad practical judgment.
Ricky will protect his pride first, then pivot fast into explanation, bluff, or plain concession depending on pressure.

branching_choices.source_moment: Ricky and Fred stand over the scorched blouse and rigid stockings, then decide what kind of dinner to make.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The stockings stand by themselves. Fred admires them. You admire him for admiring them. Between the ruined blouse and the starched silk, the next bad idea arrives dressed as dinner.
branching_choices.choice_question: What kind of dinner plan do you commit to?

options.label: A
options.text: You push for the safest plain meal you can finish without showing off.
options.outcome: You look at the board, the basket, the stockings that could scratch wallpaper, and decide the house has made its point. No heroics. No seven layers. No rice that swells, hisses, or needs explaining. Fred objects in principle, because principle is cheap, but he cannot argue with the evidence standing in his hand. The plan shrinks to something plain enough to survive two amateurs and one stove. For a moment the apartment feels almost manageable. Not competent. Just smaller. The confidence in the room drops, and with it some of the danger. The joke changes shape. Less trumpet blast. More men trying to walk around their own feet without announcing it as a dance. The pressure cooker stays a possibility, not yet a fate.
options.downstream_effect: The kitchen later runs on a simpler plan, reducing spectacle but sharpening any remaining failure as plainly avoidable.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You chose control over display.
promises_made: You commit to surviving dinner rather than winning it.
key_successes_failures: The men reduce the scale of the test.

options.world_noticed_signal: The room gets quieter. Even Fred looks at the stove instead of the horizon.
options.defining_line: You stop trying to impress the house and start trying to survive it.

options.label: B
options.text: You split the work into a sensible main course and dessert and hope two half-skills make one meal.
options.outcome: You do not retreat, and you do not exactly charge. You divide. That feels smart. One man takes the main course, one man takes dessert, and together the two of you build a plan large enough to sound impressive and practical enough to excuse in advance. Fred brightens at once. Delegation is a marvelous system when nobody asks who knows what. The apartment fills with menu talk. Chicken and rice. Cake. Frosting. The words come out smoother than the methods behind them. The scorched blouse stays on the board like a footnote nobody reads twice. The stockings remain upright in the basket, silent character witnesses. The meal now has structure. So does the coming wreckage. You have organized the disaster. That helps morale. It does not help dinner.
options.downstream_effect: The men commit to pooled labor, creating a larger but still plausible kitchen operation that can later fail in layered ways.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: pressure_cooker planned_for_use=yes; rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=medium; cake planned=yes
emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You chose division of labor over retreat or bravado.
promises_made: You commit to a pooled dinner plan.
key_successes_failures: The men scale up the household test.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred squares up as if a menu were proof. The kitchen has not done anything yet, but it has already been promised too much.
options.defining_line: You make the mistake look organized.

options.label: C
options.text: You insist on a showy dinner with arroz con pollo and a big frosted cake.
options.outcome: You go the other way. If the house is going to test you, fine. It can take the full act. Chicken and rice for the main course. A big cake for dessert. Frosting with body. Fred answers like a man whose mother once owned a stove, and that is enough for both of you. The room lifts on pure male confidence. Bad evidence becomes encouragement. The scorch says you can iron. The stockings say you can launder. Therefore dinner will be magnificent. This is not reasoning. It is momentum in an undershirt. By the time you finish planning, the meal has become a promise to the whole apartment. Boy, this is going to be the best meal our wives ever had. Complete confidence. Zero ability.
options.downstream_effect: The kitchen is set on the most ambitious path, increasing the scale of later mess, pressure, and embarrassment.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: pressure_cooker planned_for_use=high_risk; rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=high; cake planned=seven_layers_with_frosting
emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You turned correction into competition.
promises_made: You promise a dinner meant to prove the wager.
key_successes_failures: The men choose the hardest household test available.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred laughs. The stockings stay rigid. Every object in the room now seems to be waiting for evening.
options.defining_line: You see bad evidence and call for a bigger stage.

Ricky likes to restore status by proposing a workable system, but under pride pressure he overreaches fast.

branching_choices.source_moment: Lucy has made a chocolate mess at the dipping slab, her nose itches, the fly circles, and the Forelady's scrutiny is closing in.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The woman beside you keeps the same rhythm. Pick up, roll, coat, set, swirl. Your side of the slab looks like a small chocolate accident. The Forelady is not yet speaking, which is worse than speaking.
branching_choices.choice_question: How do you carry the failure before authority steps in?

options.label: A
options.text: You keep dipping faster and act as if speed can bully the work into behaving.
options.outcome: You refuse the lesson the slab is trying to teach. If the chocolates are wrong, then more chocolates faster. Your hands move before your judgment can object. Cream center. Roll. Too much. Again. Set. Smear. Another. The row grows, but not in any way a candy company would welcome. Chocolate gets on your wrists, your apron, your cap, one ear. The fly returns to a station that now resembles a public attraction. The skilled worker beside you does not look over. That is somehow insulting. By the time the Forelady arrives, you have not saved your pride. You have spread it thin over twelve lopsided pieces and half the marble slab. The mess is bigger. So is the evidence that you would rather perform competence than ask for it.
options.downstream_effect: Forelady suspicion rises sharply because Lucy's failure becomes a visible backlog of overproduced bad candy rather than a contained mistake.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You protected pride by doubling down on visible failure.
promises_kept_or_broken: Lucy cannot sustain the competence she claimed.
key_successes_failures: Lucy fails at chocolate dipping.

options.world_noticed_signal: The slab tells on you before the Forelady does. Even the neat row beside you makes your side look louder.
options.defining_line: You answer the mess with more mess and call it effort.

options.label: B
options.text: You stop, admit you cannot do it right, and ask to be shown again.
options.outcome: You take your hands off the next cream center before it can join the others. That alone feels like giving up a fight. Then you say the plain thing. You cannot do it right. Show you again. The skilled worker flicks one glance your way. Tiny. Human. The Forelady hears enough to sort you at once. There is no softness in it, but there is order. The bad row ends where it is. The slab is still a mess, though a limited one, and your pride takes the full hit in one clean piece instead of being shaved away by fifteen more mistakes. It costs you status. It buys you a little structure. In this plant, that may be the closest thing to mercy.
options.downstream_effect: Lucy's failure is contained early, leading the Forelady to respond procedurally rather than punitively and setting up reassignment with less spectacle.

npc_dispositions.shift: respect for compliance +1; confidence in skill -1

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You accepted humiliation to restore order.
sacrifices_made: Lucy swallows pride and admits the work is too much.
key_successes_failures: Lucy fails at chocolate dipping.

options.world_noticed_signal: The line beside you keeps moving. Your side stops. For the first time all scene, the mess quits growing.
options.defining_line: You let the room see you fail before it can catch you pretending.

options.label: C
options.text: You step back from the slab and accept reassignment before the station gets worse.
options.outcome: You do not confess exactly, and you do not bluff exactly. You read the slab, the Forelady, the neat row beside your own lumpy little parade, and decide this station is lost. Better to move before the loss gets theatrical. You wipe your hands on the apron, which helps nothing, and make yourself available for transfer with as much dignity as chocolate permits. The Forelady does not reward the judgment, but she does not have to drag it out of you either. That matters. The bad candies remain on the marble as a finished statement. Not a triumph. Not a catastrophe. A record. You save what can still be saved, which at this point is mostly time, some face, and Ethel's chances of not being tied to your slab.
options.downstream_effect: Lucy preserves some social leverage by reading the room early, which tempers Forelady hostility and keeps the failure from spreading further.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You protected future leverage over present pride.
promises_kept_or_broken: Lucy redefines competence as adaptation rather than mastery.
key_successes_failures: Lucy fails at chocolate dipping.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Forelady's eyes move from the slab to you and find less resistance there than expected. The room stays stern, but it stops sharpening its knife.
options.defining_line: You quit the station before the station can finish humiliating you.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Forelady inspects Lucy's work and pulls her away from the dipping room toward reassignment.

Ethel will worry plainly, support Lucy if she shares risk, and lose confidence if Lucy keeps bluffing through obvious trouble.
Lucy can bluff, concede, or adapt, but she stays proud, quick, and visibly unwilling to look small.

scene_integrity.emotional_context: Humiliation under institutional pressure. No correct answer, only different costs.

branching_choices.source_moment: Late afternoon. Ricky has too many pots going in the kitchen while Lucy has already been broken at the first factory station and the wager is visibly wobbling on both sides.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The day has not ended, but the bluff has started to show through on both floors of the story. In the apartment, too many things are on the stove. In the factory, one lesson has already landed. You can still decide what tomorrow is for.
branching_choices.choice_question: What do you commit yourself to before the next round begins?

options.label: A
options.text: You decide nobody gets to crow, and you start quietly collecting proof on both sides.
options.outcome: You make a private adjustment. Not surrender. Not another speech. Just a new rule in your own head. Nobody gets to come home tonight and act like a champion. If there is going to be evidence, you will keep it. The blouse. The stockings. The chocolate on the cap. The kitchen sounds from the other side of evening. You stop thinking about victory and start thinking about balance. That does not make you kinder. It makes you ready. The wager now looks less like a contest to win and more like a case to build. Quietly. Carefully. The room image holds on strain, not collapse. Pots going. Candy residue drying. Two households of pride walking toward the same front door with their hands full.
options.downstream_effect: The next session inherits a scorekeeping mindset shaped by observation and leverage rather than open challenge or surrender.
