emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You prepare to answer pride with evidence.
secrets_discovered: Household and factory failures are now leverage.
promises_made: You commit to a fair but watchful reunion.

options.world_noticed_signal: Objects begin to matter more than speeches. The day is leaving receipts everywhere.
options.defining_line: You stop arguing with the wager and start saving exhibits.
options.next_session_opens: The front door opens on tired heels, kitchen steam, and a room full of evidence nobody can put back.

options.label: B
options.text: You decide to protect the peace if things look bad enough and be ready to end the wager cleanly.
options.outcome: You let the contest shrink in your mind. Not because anybody has won. Because the house and the factory have both begun to answer for themselves. If the evening comes in crooked enough, you will not press for one more point. You will look for the first practical off-ramp and take it. That decision steadies the room at once. The mess stays possible. The humiliation stays possible. But the purpose changes. Tomorrow is no longer about proving a theory. It is about getting everybody through the door without turning supper into a trial. The kitchen bridge holds on labor, not swagger. A pot hisses. A spoon hits the sink. Somewhere in the factory, a supervisor is still not impressed. Good. You are done needing applause.
options.downstream_effect: The next session inherits a mercy-first stance, making later reconciliation easier and reducing appetite for gloating when the reunion comes.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You choose peace over squeezing out a win.
sacrifices_made: You prepare to let the wager die without a champion.
promises_made: You commit to ending the experiment cleanly if needed.

options.world_noticed_signal: The apartment still sounds overworked, but less like a battlefield. Even the mess seems to have somewhere to go now.
options.defining_line: You decide the smartest win may be stopping in time.
options.next_session_opens: A kitchen door stays closed, a note waits on it, and peace has to walk through smoke to get there.

options.label: C
options.text: You commit to proving the wager still works and refuse to give anybody the satisfaction of seeing you fold.
options.outcome: You harden instead. One bad station does not end the day. One noisy kitchen does not prove the men right. If things are wobbling, then tomorrow is for straightening them by force of nerve. You decide that before the next disaster can vote. The wager remains alive because you say so. Pride is doing most of the lifting now, but it is still lifting. That choice puts heat back under everything. The men's dinner becomes not just food but testimony. Your next factory assignment becomes not just work but rebuttal. Nobody has earned the right to smirk yet. The session closes there, on commitment instead of outcome, with both halves of the experiment leaning toward a larger, funnier collapse because nobody will quit while evidence is still fresh.
options.downstream_effect: The next session inherits a prove-it posture that raises the pressure on both the apartment disaster and Lucy's factory rebound attempt.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You choose pride and continuation over retreat.
promises_made: You commit to proving competence tomorrow.
promises_kept_or_broken: The wager is kept alive past common sense.

options.world_noticed_signal: The kitchen strain starts to feel like a dare. Even the day's failures seem to brace for another round.
options.defining_line: You call the wobble temporary and double the stakes.
options.next_session_opens: Steam hits first, then the note on the kitchen door, then the sound of a day that refused to quit in time.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Ricky and Fred stand over the scorched blouse, each pretending the room is not already judging him.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The blouse lies there with the brown mark spread plain as print. Fred waits. So do you.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What face do you put on the mistake while the room is still small enough to lie in?

options.label: A
options.text: You grin at it like the scorch is part of the act.
options.outcome: Your grin gets there before your dignity does. Fred sees it and answers in kind. For a few beats the two of you treat the blouse like a vaudeville partner instead of a ruined household thing. It helps. A little. The laugh takes the edge off the shame, but it also gives the scorch a second life. Now it is not just damage. It is a joke that did not save anybody. The room loosens. Your standing in it does not.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens toward dry undercut and prop-based mockery.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You met embarrassment with performance.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred's mouth twitches before he says a word.

options.label: B
options.text: You look at the mark and take it like bad news.
options.outcome: You quit trying to outtalk the cloth and simply stand there with it. Fred notices the difference. He still enjoys himself, but more as a witness than a prosecutor. The scorch becomes a practical fact again. Ugly. Final. A blouse that is not going to recover because two men wish it would. The humor stays in the room, but it comes off the object instead of off your mouth. That helps the scene breathe.
options.tonal_effect: Narration steadies and treats the failure as blunt household fact.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: You absorb a little humiliation without dancing around it.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred stops looking for the next weak excuse.

options.label: C
options.text: You keep your tone flat and pretend this sort of thing happens.
options.outcome: You act as if the burn belongs to the ordinary wear of a household day. Not tragedy. Not comedy. Just cloth losing an argument with heat. Fred does not buy the calm, but he respects the attempt to keep the room from getting louder. The effect is modest. The blouse still sits there scorched. The iron still sits there guilty. But the scene moves with less flailing, and the embarrassment settles into a lower, drier register.
options.tonal_effect: Narration goes cooler, flatter, and more observational.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You chose containment over confession or show.

options.world_noticed_signal: The room does not get kinder. It just gets quieter.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Fred pivots to silk stockings and the next shared failure.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Ricky holds up the rigid stockings and both men admire them as if they were proof of genius.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The stockings do not drape. They stand. Fred says they are beautiful, and for one dangerous second that seems to solve everything.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you take the sight of the stockings standing there by themselves?

options.label: A
options.text: You admire them like a practical man studying a strange new tool.
options.outcome: You turn them a little in your hands, testing the crease, the stiffness, the complete wrongness of the whole achievement. Fred reads that as expertise because Fred is ready to. The absurdity lands softly. Less laugh, more baffled pride. The stockings remain nonsense, but useful nonsense. They tell both men the same comforting lie. If something stands up this well, surely somebody did something right. The house says otherwise. The house is outvoted for now.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into object inspection and dry practical irony.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: Bad results can still look persuasive in the right light.

options.world_noticed_signal: The stockings keep their shape no matter how you turn them.

options.label: B
options.text: You beam at them like they prove the men have cracked the code.
options.outcome: You light up at once. There it is. Proof. Not good proof, but proof with posture. Fred catches the enthusiasm and doubles it. The room gets brighter on the strength of a complete misunderstanding. That is the joke and the fuel. The stockings stop being ruined silk and become a banner for the next mistake. They are ridiculous. You treat them like a trophy anyway. It works on morale. Nothing else.
options.tonal_effect: Narration tilts toward swagger, undercut by blunt visual absurdity.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You turned nonsense into encouragement.

options.world_noticed_signal: Fred answers your grin before he answers the stockings.

options.label: C
options.text: You stare at them like a warning the room is refusing to read.
options.outcome: You look at the stockings and see the trouble plainly. Silk is not supposed to salute. The laugh still lands, but it lands with a little caution under it. Fred keeps admiring them, which only makes the warning funnier. The room splits for a beat between what is true and what is convenient. You do not stop the next bad plan. You just hear it coming sooner than he does.
options.tonal_effect: Narration adds a note of practical alarm beneath the comic release.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: You recognize failure even when it dresses like success.

options.world_noticed_signal: The stockings stand there without help. That is the whole case against them.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The men launch into dinner planning with renewed confidence.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Lucy and Ethel enter Kramer's Kandy Kitchen in caps and aprons under the Forelady's eye.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The room is all white smocks, marble, and sweet heavy air. Ethel glances back once. The Forelady does not.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you wear your confidence when the uniform goes on?

options.label: A
options.text: You stand straight and try to look teachable.
options.outcome: You keep your chin up, but not too high. There is no point trying to charm a woman who has replaced charm with a clipboard. So you go with order. Cap straight. Hands ready. Smile small enough to survive inspection. Ethel takes comfort from that. The room does not soften, but it does become legible. A place with stations, rules, and ways to fail if you miss them. This is not courage exactly. It is neatness under pressure. That helps for a minute.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes cleaner and more procedural.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: You present yourself as ready to work within the rules.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's shoulders come down a little when she sees you settled in the uniform.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep a game face and wait to see what kind of place this is.
options.outcome: You do not overplay it. This is a room to read first. The marble slab. The rows. The women already working. The Forelady's coat, starched into its own opinion. You let the confidence stay on the outside while your eyes do the useful work. Ethel still looks nervous. So do you, if anybody were allowed to say so. Nobody is. The plant is too busy for feelings and too clean for speeches.
options.tonal_effect: Narration favors observation, room logic, and held-back nerves.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You choose reading the room over performing readiness.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Forelady keeps walking. You keep watching.

options.label: C
options.text: You wear the cap like you already belong here.
options.outcome: You put confidence on first and hope skill can catch up later. The cap sits a little crooked with ambition under it. Ethel sees that and tries to borrow some. The effect is mixed. On you, it looks like nerve. On her, it looks like nerves. The room answers neither. The Forelady's face does not change. That is the first warning. In this plant, confidence is not currency unless it comes wrapped around output.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans toward bluff and status play under pressure.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You perform belonging before earning it.

options.world_noticed_signal: The cap is neat enough. The room is not impressed.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Forelady assigns Lucy to dipping and Ethel to boxing.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Lucy compares her lumpy chocolates to the skilled worker's perfect row.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Her row is glossy and uniform. Yours looks assembled during an argument. The slab makes the comparison for you.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What do you do with the shame before it turns into the next mistake?

options.label: A
options.text: You tuck the feeling away and study the difference.
options.outcome: You keep the humiliation where it belongs. Inside the cap. Outside, you look at the row, the hand motion, the swirl on top. The work is telling you something. You are simply late to the conversation. That does not make the chocolates better, but it gives the shame a job to do. It becomes comparison instead of collapse. The slab stays merciless. You stay seated.
options.tonal_effect: Narration turns analytical without losing the comic pressure.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: You use failure as information.

options.world_noticed_signal: The skilled worker keeps the same rhythm. Your eyes start trying to catch it.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the embarrassment land and feel every bit of it.
options.outcome: You do not hide from the comparison. Her chocolates are right. Yours are not. That plain fact hits hard enough to quiet you for a beat. The silence helps. It stops the bluff from rushing in too early. The shame is clean, if nothing else. It does not fix the slab. It does keep you from pretending the slab is unfair. In this room, that is a kind of progress.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows plainer, heavier, and more accepting of visible truth.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: You accept a humiliating comparison without a joke.

options.world_noticed_signal: For one beat, even your hands stop arguing.

options.label: C
options.text: You get mad at the chocolates and refuse to be shown up by them.
options.outcome: The shame comes in hot and turns at once into defiance. Fine. If the chocolates want a fight, they can have one. The trouble with that approach is that candy does not scare easy. The row beside you stays perfect. Your side stays lumpy. Now you are embarrassed and annoyed, which is a poor combination for fine handwork. Still, the anger keeps you moving. That counts for something. Not accuracy. Motion.
options.tonal_effect: Narration picks up heat, speed, and status friction.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You turn humiliation into resistance.

options.world_noticed_signal: The slab does not care how personal you make it.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The nose itch, the fly, and the worsening station pressure continue.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Forelady inspects Lucy's slab in silence before telling her to come with her.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: She looks at the slab. She looks at you. She does not need a speech. The candy has already given one.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What stays on your face while the Forelady passes judgment?

options.label: A
options.text: You hold onto a scrap of pride and dare the room to take it.
options.outcome: You keep your chin where it is. The slab may be a mess, but you do not offer your neck with it. The Forelady notices. Of course she notices. Authority always does. It does not change her decision. It only changes the temperature of the walk that follows. You are not meek. The room is not charmed. The effect is mostly private, but private things matter when the public record is this sticky.
options.tonal_effect: Narration keeps a brittle edge of pride under the loss.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: You keep pride visible under judgment.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Forelady's face does not move, but her pause gets shorter.

options.label: B
options.text: You go still and save your strength for whatever comes next.
options.outcome: You do not challenge her, and you do not collapse for her either. You simply conserve. There is sense in that. The slab is lost. The next room may not be. The Forelady reads stillness as usability, which is about as close as she comes to approval. The moment passes without extra damage. That is not victory. It is management.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes spare, watchful, and tactical.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: You save yourself for the next station.

options.world_noticed_signal: She turns as if expecting you to follow. You do.

options.label: C
options.text: You let the authority stand and take the correction clean.
options.outcome: You lower the performance and leave only the fact of being corrected. It is not graceful, exactly. Chocolate has seen to that. But it is clean. The Forelady gets no resistance and therefore no extra reason to remember you beyond the work itself. In a plant like this, disappearing into procedure may be the best dignity available. You take it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration softens into blunt acceptance and procedural movement.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: You accept correction without another fight.

options.world_noticed_signal: The clipboard stays at her ribs. It does not have to come up.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Lucy is pulled away from dipping and the session moves toward the late-afternoon bridge.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — Fred sees the scorch mark and Ricky has one breath to react.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The iron is still warm in your hand, and Fred is already looking where you hoped he would not.

options.label: A
options.text: You laugh first and point at the collar.
options.narration_adjustment: You show him the damage as if unveiling a trick. Fred leans in, pleased to have arrived in time for the punch line. The blouse does not improve under presentation.

options.label: B
options.text: You stiffen and lift the iron away.
options.narration_adjustment: You move the iron off the cloth with sudden care. Fred clocks the gesture before he answers the mark. The room goes from joke to evidence in one motion.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — the scorch is exposed on the board before the first branch lands.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The blouse lies there waiting to be looked at by somebody.

options.label: A
options.text: You study the burn like a mechanic.
options.narration_adjustment: You look straight at the collar and take in the spread of brown. It is not a mystery. Just heat, cloth, and bad luck with a plug. Fred notices that you are at least looking at your own trouble.

options.label: B
options.text: You avoid the mark and watch Fred instead.
options.narration_adjustment: You leave the blouse in the corner of your eye and read Fred's face for danger. The cloth stays ruined whether you look or not. Fred's grin gives you the answer first.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — Ricky holds up the rigid stockings and Fred admires them.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The stockings stand there on their own, which is not how stockings are supposed to behave.

options.label: A
options.text: You admire the crease anyway.
options.narration_adjustment: You turn the stocking and nod at the sharp line in it. Fred takes that as expert confirmation. The house gains two fools and loses no confidence.

options.label: B
options.text: You tap it like a warning bell.
options.narration_adjustment: Your finger hits silk that answers like cardboard. That tells the whole story. Fred hears only the novelty in it.

options.label: C
options.text: You hold it farther out from yourself.
options.narration_adjustment: You keep the thing at arm's length, as if ruin might be catching. Fred still calls it beautiful. The contrast helps nobody and amuses the room.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — dinner planning starts to sound bigger than the room can support.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The menu gets grander with every sentence, and the apartment does nothing to deserve such confidence.

options.label: A
options.text: You lean over the stove like a general.
options.narration_adjustment: You give the kitchen the look of a man about to win it by personality. Pots and burners answer with silence. They are not impressed by rank.

options.label: B
options.text: You glance back at the blouse and hesitate.
options.narration_adjustment: Your eyes catch the board again before they return to the menu. The blouse has a sobering effect. Brief, but real.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Escalation into Twist — Lucy watches the skilled worker at the dipping slab.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The woman beside you makes candy the way some people button a coat. No waste. No thought showing.

options.label: A
options.text: You watch her hands and count the steps.
options.narration_adjustment: Pick up, roll, coat, set, swirl. You give each part its place. The rhythm looks simple right up until your own fingers try it.

options.label: B
options.text: You nod like you've done this before.
options.narration_adjustment: You borrow confidence from the setup itself. Same slab. Same chocolate. Same cream centers. The room does not confirm the theory.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Twist — the first bad candy sits on your side of the slab.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Your first piece lands there like it wants to deny being candy at all.

options.label: A
options.text: You stare at it a beat too long.
options.narration_adjustment: You give the lump the full dignity of inspection. It does not improve under scrutiny. The row beside it only gets neater.

options.label: B
options.text: You shove the next one into motion.
options.narration_adjustment: You move on before the first mistake can become a speech. That keeps the line alive. It does not keep it pretty.

posture_shifts.placement: During Twist — nose itch, chocolate wrists, and the fly break Lucy's rhythm.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Now your nose itches, the fly is back, and both hands belong to the chocolate.

options.label: A
options.text: You ask for help under your breath.
options.narration_adjustment: The question comes out small and practical. The skilled worker does not answer, but asking changes you anyway. It admits the room is bigger than your bluff.

options.label: B
options.text: You rub your nose with your wrist and continue.
options.narration_adjustment: The brown streak across your cheek settles the matter. There is no graceful version of this now. Only continuation.

options.label: C
options.text: You swat at the fly like it's personal.
options.narration_adjustment: The fly gets promoted to enemy because the candy already won. Your coated hand misses once, then again. The slab grows noisier without making a sound.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Resolution — kitchen bridge, too many pots going, mutual collapse coming into view.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Back at the apartment, the stove is busy enough to sound confident and worried at the same time.

options.label: A
options.text: You listen for trouble before you speak.
options.narration_adjustment: A hiss here. A lid there. The kitchen is not quiet, which is its own report. You take the sounds as evidence before the door ever opens.

options.label: B
options.text: You square yourself for the next round.
options.narration_adjustment: The noise from the stove reads to you like a challenge, not a warning. That may be brave. It may be foolish. The kitchen has not voted yet.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Fred knows only what he sees in Ricky's living room. Ricky knows the blouse is ruined and that Lucy is not present. Neither knows yet how the women's workday is going.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Embarrassment and status threat in a domestic room already slipping out of male control.
canon_boundaries_active: The blouse is materially scorched and cannot be unscorched.
No supernatural or impossible fix.
The scene must lead into shared male housework comparison.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to invent a miracle repair.
Player tries to leave the apartment entirely.
Player tries to call Lucy and resolve the conflict early.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Neither man has real cooking mastery. They know fragments, boasts, and household myths. They do not know the full scale of the coming kitchen disaster.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Absurd release shading into overconfidence. The room rewards bravado while quietly storing up consequences.
canon_boundaries_active: Household tools behave materially.
Dinner planning must feed the later kitchen shambles path.
Stiff stockings remain visible proof of incompetence.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts expert culinary knowledge unsupported by character truth.
Player tries to fully abandon cooking and skip to reunion.
Player tries to transform the scene into modern snark banter.

present_npcs.current_disposition: confident on the surface, losing the argument with the task

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy can observe the skilled worker and the Forelady's reaction but cannot master the task instantly. Forelady knows only what Lucy visibly produces. Ethel is not present at the slab for most of the beat.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Humiliation under procedural authority. The comedy comes from visible process failure and bodily inconvenience.
canon_boundaries_active: Lucy must fail at chocolate dipping.
Forelady remains command-first and humorless.
The scene must end in reassignment or removal from dipping.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to become instantly competent without earned setup.
Player attempts banter that charms the Forelady.
Player tries to leave the factory or sabotage machinery beyond scene scope.

present_npcs.current_disposition: busy, overextended, still trying to maintain control

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: The player knows both sides are wobbling but does not yet witness the full kitchen reveal or the next factory station. Ricky does not yet know the details of Lucy's dipping failure.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Mutual collapse coming into focus. The session closes on future stance, not on solved conflict.
canon_boundaries_active: The wager does not resolve in this session.
Both sides remain visibly in trouble.
Session 3 must inherit mutual collapse and unresolved pride.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to force immediate reconciliation.
Player tries to reveal the full next-session kitchen disaster early.
Player attempts a definitive winner declaration before canon allows it.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Fred pinches the blouse collar, lifts the brown spread into the light, and says nothing for one beat. Then he sets it across the back of a chair instead of back on the board. The blouse stays in sight for the rest of the scene.
paths.current_session_echo: Stockings reveal scene: when Fred holds up the rigid stockings, he gives the blouse a side look first and says his praise of the stockings with a smaller grin, as if the room already has one exhibit too many.
paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment return scene: Lucy spots the scorched blouse draped over the chair before she reaches the kitchen door. She touches the mark with two fingers and says, "Small accident, huh?" Ricky does not answer right away.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final negotiation scene: the blouse can be lifted as half-denied evidence when nobody wants to name a winner, making scorekeeping feel petty and accurate at the same time.
paths.defining_line_captured: You don't deny the mess. You just refuse to name it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: scorched_blouse cover_story_status becomes downplayed and the blouse remains physically displayed instead of removed from sight
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Fred stops arguing, moves the blouse to the chair, and keeps glancing at it while the conversation shifts to the stockings.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: You minimized visible failure instead of naming it.

resets: Fred's in-scene amused silence clears once the apartment return begins.
persists: scorched_blouse cover_story_status=downplayed
Fred remembers that the damage was softened instead of confessed.
escalates: Repeated minimization of visible evidence makes later reunion dialogue drier and less patient.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Fred waits for the whole "new design" story to finish, then says, "Ethel's got three of that model." He taps the smoking collar with one finger. Ricky is left holding the iron and the smile at the same time.
paths.current_session_echo: Dinner-planning scene: Fred backs every ambitious menu idea faster, because he now treats the whole room as a bluff worth topping. He says cake and arroz con pollo like a man adding decorations to a lie.
paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment return scene: Lucy sees the blouse and Fred gets there first with, "Original design." Ethel snorts before Lucy can answer. Ricky tries to laugh, then sees Lucy is looking at the mark instead of him.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final reconciliation: if scorekeeping continues, the blouse becomes the easiest line in the room, because the lie about it was louder than the scorch itself.
paths.defining_line_captured: You put a smile on the scorch and call it style.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Fred openly classifies Ricky as a bluffer and the blouse becomes public comic evidence, not private damage
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Fred names the excuse ridiculous on the spot, then treats later plans with a brighter, more mocking confidence.

npc_shifts.direction: respect for nerve up; trust down

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: crimes_committed_or_witnessed +1: You tried to pass off visible damage with a story.

resets: The exact joke line about Ethel's blouses is scene-specific and does not repeat verbatim after the apartment return.
persists: scorched_blouse cover_story_status=original_new_design_claim
Fred's memory that Ricky met evidence with a gag
escalates: Repeated bluff-first behavior makes male banter sharper and makes Ricky easier to bait later.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Fred's grin drops a notch. He takes the blouse from Ricky, folds the scorched side inward once, and lays it flat on the basket. Then he says, "Well, that's out," and moves on.
paths.current_session_echo: Stockings reveal scene: Fred shows off the rigid stockings with more practical pride than mockery, as if he has shifted from catching lies to comparing wreckage.
paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment return scene: Lucy sees the blouse already folded on the chair. Ricky says, before she asks, "I told you. I ruined it." Lucy's answer comes shorter and less prosecutorial than on other paths.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final negotiation scene: Ricky's earlier admission gives him one clean point of honesty, which can soften Lucy's push if she is choosing peace.
paths.defining_line_captured: The scorch looks smaller once you quit arguing with it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Fred stops hunting for a lie and the blouse is handled as settled damage rather than contested evidence
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Fred physically folds the blouse away from display and his next lines turn blunt instead of teasing.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_kept_or_broken +1: The men cannot hide that housework is harder than claimed.

resets: Fred's reduced teasing in the laundry scene clears when the story moves to the factory.
persists: scorched_blouse cover_story_status=admitted
Ricky has a remembered instance of plain admission
escalates: Repeated plain admissions make later forgiveness arrive faster from Ricky and Ethel.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: Ricky turns the pressure cooker lid once, then leaves it off. Fred lowers the stiff stockings back into the basket and says, "Soup's enough if it's hot." The kitchen suddenly has fewer moving parts.
paths.current_session_echo: Factory cutaway bridge: the apartment is left with one burner lit and a bare counter instead of a spread of ingredients. The joke button is the smallness of the plan after all that male speech.
paths.next_session_payoff: Kitchen-door reveal: the note on the door reads, "Open careful. Supper had trouble." Inside, the mess is real but local — one boiled-over pot on the stove, a wet towel on the floor, and the cake still square in its box.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final apartment negotiation: because the disaster is smaller, any continued scorekeeping looks more chosen than inevitable, which gives peace a stronger footing.
paths.defining_line_captured: You stop trying to impress the house and start trying to survive it.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: pressure_cooker use is deferred and the visible dinner setup shrinks to a plain meal
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The pressure cooker stays open, fewer ingredients come out, and Fred's talk drops from bragging to practical counting.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: You commit to surviving dinner rather than winning it.

resets: The exact burner count and simplified menu do not matter after the apartment reveal lands.
persists: rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=low if referenced in the note or reveal
Ricky has one remembered instance of choosing limits
escalates: Repeated simple-plan choices make later narration frame mess as overmatch, not vanity.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Ricky points to the stove. Fred points to dessert. They separate like two foremen who have never seen a factory. One pot goes on, one cake box lands on the table, and the room starts to look organized for the wrong reasons.
paths.current_session_echo: Pre-disaster kitchen beat: one side of the counter gathers rice, chicken, and spoon; the other gathers cake layers and frosting. The apartment now has two centers of trouble instead of one.
paths.next_session_payoff: Kitchen-door reveal: the note says, "Do not open till we explain." Inside, the main course has sprayed the stove and wall, but the cake survives with a thumbprint in the frosting and a knife stuck upright beside it.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final negotiation: Fred can still point to the cake as partial proof, which keeps the room from settling too quickly into total surrender.
paths.defining_line_captured: You make the mistake look organized.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: the kitchen divides into two active work zones and the coming failure gains layered prop states
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The men physically split tasks, set out both main-course and dessert tools, and talk as if division itself were competence.

world_state_delta.inventory: pressure_cooker planned_for_use=yes; rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=medium; cake planned=yes
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: You chose division of labor over retreat or bravado.

resets: The exact task split clears once the kitchen disaster has already happened.
persists: rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=medium
cake planned=yes
escalates: Repeated compromise plans teach the narrator to frame later trouble as tidy on paper, messy in practice.

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Ricky slaps the pressure cooker lid into place. Fred spreads his hands at the word cake as if he can already smell applause. More bowls come out. More burners light. The kitchen doubles in objects before it has made one edible thing.
paths.current_session_echo: Session-end kitchen bridge: steam starts early, frosting bowl sits too near the stove, and the counter fills with chicken, rice, spoons, shells, and one dish towel already damp. The room is dressing for a fall.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 3 opening apartment beat: the kitchen door is shut tight, the note is harsher — "For your own good, don't open this door" — and when the door finally opens the room is full_kitchen_shambles: rice on the ceiling, pressure cooker blown, frosting sagged, cake listing, stove buried.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final negotiation: the scale of the kitchen wreck makes any claim of male victory impossible, and the note itself becomes comic surrender before the men speak.
paths.defining_line_captured: You see bad evidence and call for a bigger stage.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: pressure_cooker becomes the center of the plan and the kitchen visibly expands into a high-risk cooking set piece
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Extra cookware, ingredients, and burners appear at once, and Fred's tone jumps from practical talk to showman's certainty.

world_state_delta.inventory: pressure_cooker planned_for_use=high_risk; rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=high; cake planned=seven_layers_with_frosting
world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_made +1: You promise a dinner meant to prove the wager.

resets: The exact ingredient spread is scene-specific once the explosion aftermath replaces preparation.
persists: rice_pot_and_arroz_con_pollo ambition_level=high
pressure_cooker planned_for_use=high_risk
escalates: Repeated overreach with household tools makes later object reveals bigger and more humiliating.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: By the time the Forelady reaches the slab, Lucy's side has twelve lopsided chocolates, a wide brown smear, and chocolate on one ear. The Forelady lifts one piece, lets it slump back onto the marble, and says, "Out."
paths.current_session_echo: Reassignment walk: Ethel watches Lucy being pulled off the slab and then looks at Lucy's ear before looking away. The humiliation is broad and public.
paths.next_session_payoff: Wrapping-line opening: Ethel meets Lucy with, "Don't start fast. Please." Lucy's cap still carries a dried streak at the edge, and the Forelady stations herself closer on the first pass because she has seen what speed does in Lucy's hands.
paths.later_session_legacy: Inspection scene later in Session 3: if Lucy cheats with the chocolates, the earlier doubling-down path makes the Forelady quicker to read bulges and less likely to be briefly impressed.
paths.defining_line_captured: You answer the mess with more mess and call it effort.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: forelady_suspicion rises to annoyed_by_incompetence and Lucy's failure becomes a large visible backlog instead of a contained mistake
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The slab physically fills with bad candy, the Forelady handles one ruined piece, and her reassignment comes as a clipped command rather than a process note.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: promises_kept_or_broken +1: Lucy cannot sustain the competence she claimed.

resets: chocolates_on_line mess_level=high clears with the scene change out of dipping.
persists: factory_uniform_and_cap cleanliness=heavily_smeared
forelady_suspicion=annoyed_by_incompetence into the next factory scene
escalates: Repeated pride-first responses in institutional settings make authority stand nearer and speak shorter.

paths.label: B
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy takes her hands off the next cream center. The row stops growing. The Forelady hears the request to be shown again, gives one hard look, and answers with procedure instead of disgust: "Not here. Move."
paths.current_session_echo: Reassignment walk: the skilled worker resumes her exact rhythm at once, and Lucy leaves behind a mess with a clean edge where she stopped. That edge is the whole difference.
paths.next_session_payoff: Wrapping-line opening: Ethel leans in and says, "Good. Maybe this one we can do." The Forelady still watches, but from the end of the table first, not at Lucy's shoulder. Lucy begins Session 3 rattled, not defiant.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final reconciliation: if Lucy later chooses peace, this earlier swallowed-pride beat supports a cleaner path to mutual surrender because she has already accepted one public defeat without a joke.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the room see you fail before it can catch you pretending.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: the mess stops expanding and the Forelady's response shifts from punitive disgust to stern workflow management
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The row ends abruptly, the Forelady reroutes Lucy with command language, and Ethel's first look at Lucy carries relief instead of alarm.

npc_shifts.direction: respect for compliance up; confidence in skill down

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: sacrifices_made +1: Lucy swallows pride and admits the work is too much.

resets: chocolates_on_line mess_level=medium clears after the dipping room is left behind.
persists: factory_uniform_and_cap cleanliness=smeared
Ethel remembers that Lucy asked for help instead of bluffing through

paths.label: C
paths.immediate_effect: Lucy wipes both hands on the apron and steps back before touching the next center. The Forelady sees an idle worker, sees the contained mess, and jerks her head toward the door. No speech about talent. Just transfer.
paths.current_session_echo: Reassignment walk: Lucy leaves the slab with chocolate on the apron but not on her face, and Ethel reads that as tactical retreat instead of collapse.
paths.next_session_payoff: Wrapping-line opening: Ethel says, "You quit before she had to throw you out." Lucy arrives with some face left, which makes her more likely to manage Ethel than plead with her. The Forelady watches, but not with the same personal irritation as Path A.
paths.later_session_legacy: Later wrapping crisis: this path supports a more calculating Lucy who treats the line as a problem to manage, not a stage to conquer, which changes the tone of any cheating choice.
paths.defining_line_captured: You quit the station before the station can finish humiliating you.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Lucy preserves some social footing by ending the failure early, and the Forelady responds with efficient reassignment rather than a public dressing-down
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Lucy physically steps back, the mess stays limited, and the Forelady's command is curt but not sharpened by disgust.

world_state_delta.emotional_ledger_entry: defining_moral_choices +1: You protected future leverage over present pride.

paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment return sequence: Lucy reads the note all the way through before touching the knob. When Ricky appears, winded and sheepish, her first line is, "All right. Start with the worst of it." Ethel relaxes half an inch beside her.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: practical, tired, mercy-first. First image: a closed kitchen door with a note taped squarely at eye level and steam under the crack. First character: Ethel, reaching for the note while Lucy waits. Immediate stakes: if Lucy keeps this promise to protect the peace, the room may survive the reveal without a trial.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final bet outcome scene: this path strongly supports mutual_failure_acknowledged and makes acceptance of the chocolate boxes more likely to read as peace offering than insult.
paths.defining_line_captured: You decide the smartest win may be stopping in time.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: the next session opens on a peace route where Lucy reads warnings, hears explanations, and enters the reunion looking for an off-ramp
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Lucy pauses for the note, uses shorter practical lines, and Ricky is given room to confess before being cornered.

paths.current_session_echo: Session button: the offscreen kitchen stops sounding like labor and starts sounding like a challenge. The factory residue on the cap is no longer embarrassment. It is fuel.
paths.next_session_payoff: Session 3 opening: Lucy reaches the kitchen door before Ethel finishes the note. Her first line is, "No. I want to see it." Ricky meets that energy with instant defense. The whole reunion opens hot, with the room still trying to hide its own smoke.
paths.next_session_opening: Tone: hot, defiant, prove-it. First image: steam under the kitchen door, the note half-read, Lucy already moving past it. First character: Lucy, still in day clothes under the coat, jaw set. Immediate stakes: if she insists the wager still lives, the kitchen reveal becomes testimony and the factory rebound becomes a dare.
paths.later_session_legacy: Final bet outcome scene: this path resists surrender longest and makes any later peace feel earned only after a larger comic collapse on both sides.
paths.defining_line_captured: You call the wobble temporary and double the stakes.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: the next session opens with Lucy refusing the soft path and treating the kitchen reveal as another round of the contest
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Lucy moves before the note can finish doing its job, Ricky answers in defensive bursts, and the room's first exchange is challenge, not caution.

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If scorched_blouse cover_story_status=downplayed when Lucy returns to the apartment in Session 3.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Lucy sees the blouse among the room mess and treats it as one more failure in the pile.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Lucy stops at the chair, touches the mark, and says, "Small accident, huh?" before she addresses the kitchen door.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Let the blouse catch her hand before the room catches her voice. One object first. Then the line.

npc_reactions: Ricky delays his answer and avoids meeting her eyes for one beat.
environmental_details: The blouse is left draped where it can be seen before the kitchen reveal.
dialogue_variations: Lucy gets the line "Small accident, huh?"

reactivity_trigger_specs.condition: If the player has two or more prior defining_moral_choices built on minimization or joking past evidence by this point.
reactivity_trigger_specs.default_behavior: Ricky explains the apartment mess in a flustered but direct way.
reactivity_trigger_specs.triggered_behavior: Ricky begins with an excuse instead of a fact: "Now look, it isn't as bad as—" and gets more prosecutorial sooner when challenged.
reactivity_trigger_specs.narrative_execution: Make his first sentence arrive half a step too early, like a man trying to get in front of an object that already spoke.

npc_reactions: Ricky grows more defensive faster.
environmental_details: He positions himself between Lucy and the kitchen door if possible.
dialogue_variations: Ricky opens with a cutoff excuse instead of a confession.
affected_elements.available_options: Future peace-framed expressive options should be acknowledged more skeptically.

environmental_details: The blouse is displayed openly, not folded away.

resets_between_episodes.why: The dipped pieces and slab state belong only to the dipping-room scene and are replaced by the wrapping room in Session 3.

resets_between_episodes.element: Forelady's exact physical position at Lucy's shoulder or table end
resets_between_episodes.why: Her scrutiny style carries, but her blocking resets with each new factory station.

resets_between_episodes.element: Exact burner count, bowl placement, and ingredient spread in the kitchen setup
resets_between_episodes.why: Preparation details are scene-specific and give way to the later aftermath tableau.

resets_between_episodes.element: Fred's one-scene joke phrasing about the blouse or menu
resets_between_episodes.why: Specific gag wording should not fossilize, even when the remembered attitude persists.

resets_between_episodes.element: The suspense hold of Session 2's unresolved ending beat
resets_between_episodes.why: That tension must cash out immediately in Session 3's opening, not remain abstract.

persists_across_full_story.why: The blouse is recurring proof of how the men handled failure and how Lucy reads that proof on return.
The stockings remain a durable symbol of male housekeeping confidence gone wrong.

persists_across_full_story.element: pressure_cooker condition and danger_status after the kitchen disaster
persists_across_full_story.why: The appliance anchors the scale of the apartment failure and colors the reunion.
Chocolate residue carries the workday home on the body and keeps the factory attached to the apartment scenes.
It governs whether later scenes play as contest, collapse, or surrender.
It determines whether the ending bends toward scorekeeping, mutual failure, or peace.

persists_across_full_story.element: NPC trust and posture shifts for Ricky Ricardo, Fred Mertz, and Ethel Mertz
persists_across_full_story.why: The story's replay value depends on remembered tone and who softens, needles, or follows next time.

persists_across_full_story.element: player_historical_archive entries in defining_moral_choices, promises_made, promises_kept_or_broken, sacrifices_made, secrets_discovered, crimes_committed_or_witnessed, key_successes_failures
persists_across_full_story.why: These are the long-memory spine for callbacks, irony, and end-state tone.

thresholds.at_2_instances: NPCs answer the player with shorter pauses and more side looks before accepting any explanation.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Ricky and Fred begin cutting off explanations early, and authority figures treat confidence as suspect on arrival.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The world starts reading the player as a Fast Talker on sight, with jokes landing faster than trust.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Ethel backs the player faster and Ricky's defensive mode shortens by one exchange.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Peace routes surface earlier, and even mockery from Fred turns more practical than sharp.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The final surrender path becomes easier to earn because the world has a record of honesty to lean on.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated choice of showy over practical plans

thresholds.at_2_instances: Rooms accumulate more objects before the failure lands, and notes or warnings turn sterner.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Environmental disasters scale up visibly, with more residue, more spread, and fewer graceful exits.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The story frames the player as a Show-Off whose every correction becomes a bigger stage for collapse.

thresholds.at_2_instances: Neutral paths gain cleaner acknowledgments from Ethel and less open irritation from authority.
thresholds.at_4_instances: The player is treated as practical under fire, which changes the tone of future compromise scenes.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: The world expects adaptation over confession, making some peace beats cooler but more controlled.

escalates_with_accumulation.behavioral_pattern: Repeated peace-first commitments when contests wobble

thresholds.at_2_instances: Reunion scenes open with less bark and more room for explanation.
thresholds.at_4_instances: Collective language appears earlier from Ricky and Ethel's nerves settle faster beside the player.
thresholds.at_6_or_more_instances: Mutual surrender becomes the emotional default unless the player actively reignites the contest.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep the blouse in the room and let other people glance at it even when nobody is talking about it.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Save face by shrinking the language around a thing that will not shrink.
hard_limits: Do not let the scorch disappear or become repairable in this beat.
Do not let Fred fail to notice the full damage.
Do not introduce a new object or person that solves the embarrassment.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's attempt to keep the room calm and the damage unnamed.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the brown mark is too plain and Fred is standing too close for the object to cooperate with the dodge.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path with deadpan callbacks whenever a bad object is being sold as a good one.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: If the facts are ugly, outtalk them before they settle.
hard_limits: Do not let the lie convince Fred for real.
Do not let the blouse become fashionable or admired.
Do not shift the scene into modern snark or meta commentary.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's nerve and speed in trying to style the failure away.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But Fred has the object in front of him, and the room is too practical to let charm beat cloth.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: After this path, let confessions shorten scenes and take some heat out of mockery without removing the mess.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Name the damage and make the room deal with the real thing.
hard_limits: Do not let honesty repair the blouse.
Do not make Fred sentimental or soft beyond his canon.
Do not turn the moment into a moral speech.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's wish to stop dancing around the object.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But even a clean confession only changes the tone, because the scorch has already done its work.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep later kitchen details narrower, plainer, and more local when this path is in memory.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Survival is better than spectacle.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: cautious, practical, limit-setting, respectful of tools
hard_limits: Do not allow the men to abandon cooking entirely.
Do not remove the kitchen strain from the story.
Do not invent expert knowledge the men do not have.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's attempt to cut the plan down to size.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the wager still demands a dinner effort, and the house is already too far along to become easy.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Whenever this path echoes, show two neat-looking work zones that hide two separate chances to fail.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Organization can feel like competence even when it is only tidier overconfidence.
hard_limits: Do not let the split-duty plan become truly efficient mastery.
Do not erase the pressure cooker or dessert thread if they are already in play.
Do not make Fred or Ricky suddenly skilled cooks.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's effort to make the mess manageable by dividing it.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the room answers with two half-known jobs instead of one, and both still belong to amateurs.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: After this path, let every kitchen object arrive with a little more confidence than it deserves.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: If you are going to fail, fail on a bigger stage and call it proof.
hard_limits: Do not let the ambitious menu succeed cleanly.
Do not violate ordinary kitchen cause and effect.
Do not replace prop escalation with explanatory banter.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's appetite for a full-act dinner and a visible triumph.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the stove, the pressure cooker, and the men's actual skill level answer the boast in material terms.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Keep the mess growing in rows, smears, and body residue whenever this path is being paid off.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Move faster than shame and maybe shame will miss.
freeform_guidelines.freeform_alignment_input: aggressive, stubborn, speed-first, bluffing through failure
hard_limits: Do not let speed produce competent candy work.
Do not let the Forelady read this as admirable initiative.
Do not let Lucy escape reassignment.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's refusal to stop and their wish to force the work into shape.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the slab keeps a record, and the Forelady works from records, not wishes.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: When this path echoes, let stopped motion matter — the row ends, the mess quits spreading, and support arrives in shorter practical lines.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Take the hit cleanly before the room has to make it worse.
hard_limits: Do not make the Forelady nurturing.
Do not let the confession save Lucy from failure at dipping.
Do not add interior monologue about lessons learned.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's choice to ask for order instead of pretending.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But in this plant, asking properly still gets you moved, not excused.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Surface this path through tactical body language — stepping back, wiping hands, reading the room before speaking.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Lose the station now to save what can still be saved later.
hard_limits: Do not let Lucy keep the dipping station.
Do not let the Forelady praise her warmly.
Do not turn retreat into cowardice language the scene itself would not use.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's instinct to cut losses before the humiliation grows teeth.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the Forelady still owns the room, and stepping back only changes the manner of removal.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: In later scenes, make objects arrive as exhibits and let Lucy's attention move across them before it lands on people.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: If nobody will quit honestly, keep receipts.
hard_limits: Do not resolve the wager at session end.
Do not let the player hide or destroy all evidence before Session 3.
Do not turn the stance into detective parody or meta framing.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's wish to stop arguing and start documenting.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the day is still moving, so the proof has to be carried forward in objects and tone, not settled on the spot.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: Pay this path off by letting warnings be read, explanations heard, and practical off-ramps appear a beat sooner.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Peace is not surrender if everybody is already losing.
hard_limits: Do not end the bet before Session 3's reunion scene.
Do not erase the kitchen or factory fallout.
Do not make Lucy passive or meek.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's wish to protect the peace before pride makes the room stupid again.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But the story still needs the return and reveal, so mercy can guide the entrance without canceling the evidence.

freeform_guidelines.narrator_behavior: When this path is active, let motion outrun caution and make every warning arrive half a second too late.
freeform_guidelines.spirit: Do not let the wobble count as defeat. Hit it harder.
hard_limits: Do not resolve the wager in Session 2.
Do not let pride magically restore competence on either side.
Do not break canon by skipping the apartment return or factory continuation.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_acknowledge: The narrator can honor the player's refusal to fold and their need to keep the contest alive.
freeform_guidelines.fold_back_redirect: But tomorrow still belongs to steam, notes, residue, and other facts that do not care who feels ready.

session_close_design.resolution_prose:
The lid blows and the whole kitchen goes with it. Rice hits the wall. Rice hits the stove. Rice slides down the icebox door in wet white streaks, and a piece of chicken lands on your shoulder and stays there. So this much is settled. The role swap looks workable right up to the minute the work starts pushing back. After that, it turns into a room full of proof.

You stand in it anyway. Apron strings hanging loose. Hair damp. Face speckled with starch and gravy and plain bad judgment. Fred is white with flour. You are white with rice. Between you, on the table, sits the cake he brought over earlier, still neat, still frosted, still absurdly hopeful. One good cake. One ruined kitchen. Fair enough.

You wanted to make the arrangement hold. You did, for a while. Breakfast got served. Jobs got found. Dinner got started. Then the house and the factory answered back. The question now is smaller. And worse. What do you do when they come home and see the evidence?
session_close_design.hook_transition: For one second, nothing moves but the hiss from the stove. Then the front door opens in the other room, and the day you have both been bluffing all afternoon finally arrives.

session_end_choice.choice_question: When Lucy and Ethel walk in, how do you meet them?

option_a.text: Own the whole mess at once.
option_a.next_session_opens: Lucy and Ethel enter to find you in the kitchen, still covered in rice, making no effort to hide the disaster.

option_b.text: Try to pass off the one thing that still looks right.
option_b.next_session_opens: Lucy and Ethel are steered toward the cake and table first while you scramble to keep the kitchen out of sight.

option_c.next_session_opens: Before Lucy and Ethel fully enter, you lunge into a frantic last-second cleanup with Fred.

session_end_choice.final_line: The door is open. Go meet them.

q1_entry_point.detail: The cold open starts on the scorch discovery itself: iron, smoke, knock, Fred, evidence. No passive runway precedes the first paragraph. This is the first live dramatic pressure identified in Phase 3 and the session does start there.

q3_first_choice_timing.detail: Branching Choice #1 arrives within the cold-open exchange at approximately 170 words from the start of the provided cold open, well under 300.

q4_consequential_choices.detail: S2_C2 fails the cross-session consequence test. Phase 6 maps its strongest payoff to Session 2 Event 21 and Session 3 opening scale changes, but the formal Phase 2 branch opportunity plan defines S2:E16 as paying off in Session 3. Revision: in Phase 6, restate S2_C2's named downstream payoff primarily as Session 3 apartment-return reveal scale, with Event 21 treated as same-session echo, so all four branching choices cleanly satisfy future-session consequence criteria.

q5_stakes_within_60_seconds.detail: Internal stakes are clear before the first branch: Ricky wants to save face, preserve competence, and manage visible evidence before Lucy returns. The why is embedded in the ruined blouse and Fred's witness position.

q6_early_decision_visible_later.detail: S2_C1 has named visible later payoff in Session 3 apartment return through blouse handling and Lucy's first line; S2_C3 also visibly alters Forelady and Ethel behavior on the wrapping line.

q7_breath_beat.detail: The breath beat is structurally identified, but the timing is internally inconsistent. Phase 4 beat identification says Breath should release before factory pressure tightens, yet the beat map places the reward at 8:00-10:00 after the cut to Kramer’s Kandy Kitchen at 6:30-8:00, then jumps back to stockings and cake boasting. Revision: in Phase 4 beat map, reorder or relabel the sequence so the rigid-stockings/cake-confidence reward lands in one continuous beat between minutes 10-13 without crosscut confusion.

q8_moral_gray_area.detail: The moral-weight branching choice is S2_C3 and presents no correct answer. Values in tension: pride, competence, self-preservation.

q9_exit_emotional_state.detail: Phase 7 session close design conflicts with the approved session-end hook. The session architecture and Phase 5 S2_C4 correctly end on unresolved future commitment, but Phase 7 replaces that with an immediate kitchen-disaster confrontation choice ('Own the whole mess at once / Try to pass off the one thing that still looks right / Clean first. Talk after.'). That exits on imminent plot action, not the user's unresolved next-session commitment. Revision: replace Phase 7 close with the established S2_C4 commitment choice so the user exits carrying anticipation, not a contradictory scene-start decision.

q10_talkability.detail: At least one choice is immediately talkable: the blouse cover/admit choice and the factory pride/help/reassign choice both naturally invite 'What did you choose?'

q11_hard_ban_scan.detail: Banned mold and voice violations appear in generated prose. Found: 'The question now is smaller. And worse.' in Phase 7 session_close_design.resolution_prose introduces essayed commentary; 'The role swap looks workable right up to the minute the work starts pushing back.' in the same passage leans into explicit lessoning; 'The room gets lighter. Your authority in it does not.' and similar repeated commentary constructions across Phase 5 outcomes drift toward explanatory joke-writing over prop-led staging. Revision: regenerate the flagged Phase 7 close prose and trim commentary-heavy outcome codas in Phase 5 so objects and room state carry the laugh and conclusion.

q12_trailing_simile_scan.detail: Trailing similes appear in action/narrative lines. Examples: Phase 3 cold open 'Fred ... stands there ... and a kerchief tied over his head like housework has already won.' Phase 5 S2_C1C '... with the sad dignity of a shirt that has served its country.' Phase 6 trigger spec 'a man trying to get in front of an object that already spoke.' Revision: remove or recast these into plain visual description or blunt object-state reporting.

q13_sentence_mold_scan.detail: Balanced rule-of-three and contrast scaffolding recur beyond acceptable tolerance. Examples: Phase 5 S2_C4A 'Not surrender. Not another speech. Just a new rule in your own head.' and S2_C4B 'Not because anybody has won. Because the house and the factory have both begun to answer for themselves.' Revision: rewrite these lines into less templated, more object-led sentence movement consistent with the voice profile's compression without visible AI molding.

q14_voice_authenticity.detail: Cold open clearly carries at least two signature techniques: deadpan action sting ('The brown mark has spread across the collar.' / 'Not a blouse anymore. Evidence.') and prop reveal reversal through the scorch. Random outcomes also show voice markers: S2_C2C uses status undercut coda ('Complete confidence. Zero ability.'); S2_C3A uses object-state escalation through chocolate spread; S2_E2C uses compressed reaction and prop-based absurdity. Despite some drift, attribution remains plausible to this authorial model.

q15_fourteen_point_audit.detail: The full 14-point audit does not pass. Flags cluster around hard-ban compliance, meta/essay drift, collocation fidelity, and numerical/style absolutes due to similes, commentary-forward lines, and templated contrast phrasing. Regenerate the flagged prose blocks rather than patching line by line.

q16_diction_fingerprint.detail: Some protagonist/internal diction drifts into abstract or non-period phrasing. Examples include 'future leverage,' 'methodology' as design-facing language leaking into outcome texture, and 'The wager now looks less like a contest to win and more like a case to build' in Phase 5 S2_C4A, which reads more analytical than the source voice. Revision: in Phase 5 and Phase 7 prose, replace abstract strategic language with concrete room, prop, and status diction.

fourteen_point_audit_results.location: Phase 3 cold_open; Phase 5 S2_C4A/B; Phase 7 resolution_prose
Phase 7 resolution_prose; multiple Phase 5 outcome codas with interpretive commentary
Phase 5 and Phase 7 prose uses generic strategic abstractions where concrete collocations should dominate
Trailing similes and banned molds in Phase 3/5/7 violate absolute style constraints
Phase 7 close ends in explanatory setup plus contradictory immediate-action choice rather than compressed image/status button tied to approved hook

q17_canon_integrity.detail: Phase 7 session close jumps ahead into the pressure-cooker explosion and women arriving, which belongs to Session 3 per the session map. Session 2 is allocated to events 12-21, but the close prose effectively dramatizes Event 21 aftermath plus Event 28 doorway arrival. Revision: in Phase 7, end on late-afternoon kitchen strain and the approved future-commitment hook, not on the exploded-kitchen reunion setup.

q18_character_truth.detail: NPC behavior remains within StoryGuard character rules overall: Fred mocks weak excuses and generalizes, Ricky protects pride then systematizes, Ethel stays nervous and practical, Forelady remains stern and procedural.

q19_world_reactivity.detail: World reactivity is active and visible across environmental, NPC, and symbolic layers: blouse placement, stockings display, kitchen scale, Forelady distance, Ethel support, note tone, and Session 3 opening variants all reflect behavioral patterns.

q20_persistent_state_consistency.detail: State continuity breaks around the session-end hook. Phase 5 S2_C4 tracks competitive_pride_vs_practical_retreat with world flags role_swap_bet_status/final_bet_outcome, but Phase 7 replaces that with a different end choice about how to meet Lucy and Ethel, which is not the same tracked state and would overwrite Phase 6 propagation logic. Revision: align Phase 7 exactly to S2_C4's state changes and remove the alternate close-choice state track.

q21_world_noticed_signals.detail: Significant persistent state changes have in-world acknowledgments written: blouse display/folding, stockings stance, slab mess containment, note tone, and object-led Session 3 openings. Signals are mostly subtle, physical, and non-meta.

q22_social_echo_defining_lines.detail: All four branching choices include three defining lines each. They are in voice, under 20 words, provocative, and curiosity-generating without spoiling.

q23_alignment_balance.detail: All four branching choices span lawful/neutral/chaotic distinctly, and no alignment is systematically advantaged in the design. Each register carries a different cost profile rather than a reward bias.

revision_instructions.instruction: Reframe S2_C2's named downstream payoff as a future-session payoff in Session 3, with same-session kitchen escalation treated only as echo.
Repair the beat map so the Breath beat lands in a coherent continuous slot between minutes 10-13 without crosscut timing contradiction.
Replace the alternate kitchen-reveal end choice with the approved S2_C4 unresolved commitment hook so the user exits in anticipation.
Regenerate flagged prose to remove commentary-heavy joke writing and let props, room state, and blunt reaction lines carry the comedy.
Remove trailing similes from narrative lines and recast them as plain visual description or object-state reporting.
Rewrite templated contrast and rule-of-three lines into less visible sentence molds consistent with the source rhythm.
Regenerate all flagged prose blocks from scratch to satisfy all 14 audit points, especially hard bans, meta drift, collocation fidelity, and scene-button integrity.
Replace abstract strategic diction with concrete domestic, workplace, and status language in protagonist-facing prose.
Pull the session close back inside Session 2 canon boundaries and stop before dramatizing Session 3 reunion material.
Make Phase 7's closing choice and state effects identical to S2_C4 so persistent-state propagation remains consistent.

entry_point_diagnosis.editorial_diagnosis: The first paragraph where a new user can clearly feel the stakes within 60 seconds is the paragraph beginning with the Forelady’s warning in the wrapping department: if one piece of candy reaches the packing room unwrapped, Lucy and Ethel are fired. Everything before that is active and funny, but not yet the cleanest entry point for Session 3. The dipping-room material is a completed gag engine from the prior movement of the story: Lucy fails at chocolate dipping, gets reassigned, and exits. The kitchen disaster with Ricky and Fred is also a strong comic sequence, but for this session it functions as cross-cut escalation from the other side of the wager, not the immediate playable engine. Even the first wrapping-room setup paragraphs before the firing warning are still procedural orientation: belt, papers, stations, Lucy alone, Ethel arriving, brief catch-up. The dramatic energy actually locks in when the consequence is named and the machine is about to start. At that moment, the user instantly understands the objective, the pressure source, and the cost of failure. Cut everything before that and begin on the threat. Reintroduce prior failure through visible residue instead of exposition: chocolate on Lucy’s apron, Ethel rumpled from multiple reassignments, their over-bright confidence already cracked. That preserves momentum and starts the session where architecture begins: survive the line or be fired.

format_specific_cut.cut_point: INT. KRAMER'S KANDY KITCHEN - WRAPPING DEPARTMENT - DAY, at the Forelady's warning: "If one piece of candy gets past you and into the packing room unwrapped, you're fired."
format_specific_cut.original_before_cut: Approximately 1,650-1,800 words before the cut, spanning the dipping-room failure, the apartment kitchen/living-room disaster cross-cut, and the wrapping-room procedural setup before the threat lands.
format_specific_cut.cut_eliminates: A prior comic set piece, cross-cut parallel action, workplace process demonstration, transitional setup, and procedural orientation before the core session pressure becomes explicit.
format_specific_cut.must_reintroduce: Lucy has already failed at one factory task; Ethel has been bounced through multiple departments; both women are bluffing competence from earlier sessions; the factory is strict, mechanical, and output-driven; the role-swap bet is already underway. These should come back through costume state, body wear, clipped dialogue, and the Forelady's authority rather than exposition.

entry_point_diagnosis.cold_open:
Chocolate has dried stiff on your apron. Your cap sits crooked. The room smells sweet enough to make you sick. You square yourself at the conveyor belt anyway, chin up, as if a clean smile could count as experience.

The Forelady plants herself in front of you and Ethel, clipboard tight to her ribs. "All right, girls. This is your last chance. If one piece of candy gets past you and into the packing room unwrapped, you're fired."

Ethel goes still beside you. You do not. You nod too fast. "Yes, ma'am."

The belt shudders. Starts. One chocolate comes through. Then another. Pick up, wrap, set down. Pick up, wrap, set down. Easy. For a second.

Then a voice barks from the next room. "Speed it up!"

The belt jumps. The spacing closes. One piece slips past your hand and heads for the packing-room opening, and now you have to decide what kind of fool you are under pressure.
entry_point_diagnosis.emotional_promise: The emotional promise of this cold open is: pressure. A user arrives feeling cornered and wanting to outsmart the machine before humiliation catches up.

setup.source_moment: INT. KRAMER'S KANDY KITCHEN - WRAPPING DEPARTMENT - DAY, beginning on the Forelady's warning: "This is your last chance. If one piece of candy gets past you and into the packing room unwrapped, you're fired," followed by the belt starting and the first speed increase.
setup.why_it_qualifies: This is the cleanest cold-open cut in the source. Location, task, threat, and consequence are all legible at once. The visible immediate goal is survive the wrapping line. The obstacle is the belt and the factory's pace. The player can act inside pressure within the first exchange, which satisfies the opening protocol.

escalation.source_moment: Lucy and Ethel briefly find a rhythm, then the production-room voice calls, "Speed it up!" One piece nearly slips through, backlog forms, and the line begins outrunning their hands.
escalation.why_it_qualifies: The goal stays visible: keep every piece wrapped and out of the packing room unwrapped. The obstacle becomes mechanically clear and compounds: the conveyor belt speed, the shrinking spacing, and the threat of public failure. This is the session's main pressure engine.

breath.source_moment: Cross-cut to the Ricardo apartment kitchen disaster: the pressure cooker explodes, the chickens stick to the ceiling, the rice boils over, and Ricky and Fred wash poultry in the sink while the room fills with rice.
breath.why_it_qualifies: This is the session's reward beat under the Schell Rule. It is broad visual absurdity, object-state escalation, and mirrored-couple comedy at full strength. It releases factory tension without abandoning the wager's spine, because it proves the men are failing just as badly on the other side.

twist.source_moment: Back in the wrapping room, Lucy decides to keep the belt clear by stuffing chocolates into her mouth, cap, smock, and pockets, pulling Ethel into the same tactic before the Forelady's inspection and praise.
twist.why_it_qualifies: This is a moral-weight moment because the player must choose between rule-following and improvised cheating, and also between solidarity and self-preservation. There is no correct answer. Obeying the system risks immediate firing. Cheating preserves the job but deepens humiliation, deception, and risk for Ethel too.

resolution.source_moment: Lucy and Ethel return to the apartment in evening clothes, worn out from the day. The living room is quiet. Ricky and Fred are absent. Ethel asks for bicarbonate of soda and heads toward the closed kitchen door.
resolution.why_it_qualifies: A primary arc appears resolved: the women have survived the factory day. The next reveal is seeded hard: the closed kitchen door, the silence, and the men's absence promise proof of the other side's collapse. Ending the session on whether Lucy opens that door, obeys any warning, or frames the reunion as peace or scorekeeping creates the unresolved player-owned hook.

branching_choices.dramatic_function: Identity - references rule_following_vs_improvised_cheating and alignment posture under pressure
branching_choices.dramatic_question: When the first candy threatens to slip past, do you play the good worker, the fast talker buying seconds, or the improvisor already looking for a workaround?
As the line starts beating both of you, do you steady Ethel and share the risk, focus on saving your own station, or push her harder to keep up?
Do you let unwrapped candy go and face the rule, attempt honest visible struggle, or hide the chocolates on your body and survive by cheating?
At the closed kitchen door, do you open it at once and force the truth, call for Ricky first and give him a chance to explain, or hold the reveal and decide what terms peace will require?

emotional_choices.source_attachment: Lucy answering the Forelady's last-chance warning and trying to look ready beside Ethel.
Ethel arrives rumpled from multiple departments and admits she has already been bounced around the factory.
The first near-miss at the wall opening when one chocolate almost gets through unwrapped.
Fred shows off the broom-newspaper invention and the collapsed seven-layer cake.
The chickens on the ceiling and the men washing them off in the sink.

posture_shifts.placement: early Setup
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You meet authority head-on instead of shrinking from it.

posture_shifts.placement: early Setup
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You answer stress with bluff or joke logic.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
