posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You protect Ethel when her nerves show.

posture_shifts.placement: mid Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You turn pressure into competitive speed.

posture_shifts.placement: late Escalation
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You notice the belt, spacing, and wall opening as practical threats.

posture_shifts.placement: during Breath beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You take pleasure in the men's collapse rather than sympathy.

posture_shifts.placement: during Twist beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You go quiet and survival-minded when shame sets in.

posture_shifts.placement: during Twist beat
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You drag Ethel into the cheat because motion matters more than dignity.

posture_shifts.placement: late Resolution
posture_shifts.player_attitude_exposed: You approach the apartment reveal ready to keep score.

content_budget.narration_token_budget: 3,400-4,300 words of narrator output across the full session
compression_targets: The pre-warning wrapping-room procedural explanation before the threat lands will be compressed into a few lines of visible setup and residue.
The apartment kitchen disaster will play as one concentrated Breath sequence rather than a long cross-cut chain.
The evening exterior brownstone beat will compress into a short transition image before the apartment return.
Factory backstory from the dipping-room failure will be reintroduced through costume state, Ethel's condition, and clipped callback lines rather than replayed in full.
expansion_targets: The Forelady's warning and first acceleration need full opening breathing room because Branching Choice 1 must land inside immediate danger.
The first backlog and near-miss at the packing-room opening should expand to support solidarity pressure with Ethel.
The concealment tactic in the wrapping room deserves full comic-moral expansion because it is the session's signature Twist.
The apartment return and closed kitchen door should expand at the end so the final unresolved choice belongs to the player.

must_survive_verbatim: "This is your last chance. If one piece of candy gets past you and into the packing room unwrapped, you're fired."
"Speed it up!"
"Listen, Ethel, I think we're fighting a losing game."
"Fine. You're doing splendidly."
"Speed it up a little!"
"What happened to my chickens?! I had two chickens in there!"
"Catch the rice! Hurry up, Fred!"
"Ricky?"
"Fred?"
can_be_reshaped: The Forelady's explanatory wrapping instructions can be shortened while preserving her command-first cadence.
Ethel's report about being kicked out of other departments can be tightened into fewer lines.
Ricky and Fred's cake and rice discussion can be compressed while keeping the deadpan object logic.
The apartment return exchange about bicarbonate of soda can be lightly reshaped for pacing.
can_be_cut: Most of the dipping-room instructional detail and skilled-worker demonstration, since this session starts after that engine.
Exterior factory establishing description beyond one practical sentence.
Redundant apartment transitional filler before Fred arrives.
Any repeated wrapping-process lines once the mechanical pattern is established.

beat_map.moment: Cold open in the wrapping department. Chocolate on Lucy's apron. Ethel beside her. The Forelady states the firing rule.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Immediate orientation. Establish place, threat, and player stance toward authority.

beat_map.moment: The belt starts. First candy comes through. First speed jump threatens an early miss.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 1. Identity under pressure.

beat_map.moment: Visible consequence of the player's opening stance. Ethel reacts. The Forelady either lingers or moves off.
beat_map.dramatic_function: First visible consequence within the opening two minutes.

beat_map.moment: A brief private exchange with Ethel about how many departments she has already failed through.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Texture choice on loyalty, nerves, and how Lucy frames shared trouble.

beat_map.moment: Pick up, wrap, set down. For a stretch it works. The women start believing they can handle it.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Expose whether the player settles, boasts, studies the system, or overreaches.

beat_map.moment: The production-room voice calls, "Speed it up!" One piece nearly reaches the packing-room opening.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 2. Solidarity vs self-preservation.

beat_map.moment: Backlog forms at both stations. Ethel's rhythm slips. The wall opening becomes a countdown.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Texture choice on panic, restraint, or encouragement.

beat_map.moment: Lucy clocks the belt, the spacing, and the fact that hands cannot win at this speed.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Micro-agency through observation or denial before the release beat.

beat_map.moment: Cut to the Ricardo apartment. Fred arrives with the broom-newspaper invention and the collapsed cake.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Reward beat. Smug amusement and mirrored-couple absurdity.

beat_map.moment: Rice math, pressure-cooker hiss, and the men's false confidence.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Let the player savor, mock, or quietly clock the incoming disaster.

beat_map.moment: The pressure cooker explodes. Chickens on the ceiling. The men wash them in the sink.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Absurd release through disbelief and comic superiority.

beat_map.moment: The rice boils over. The kitchen becomes a white tide. Nothing left to say.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Button the reward beat on object-state escalation and status collapse.

beat_map.moment: Back to the wrapping room. Chocolates pile up faster than hands can clear them. Lucy sees no legal way out.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Pre-choice pressure. Expose whether the player freezes, calculates, or hardens.

beat_map.moment: Lucy decides how to survive the line: obey, fail honestly, or cheat by concealment.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 3. Rule-following vs improvised cheating.

beat_map.moment: If cheating, Ethel must be brought in. If not, both women face the visible cost. Their bodies become the consequence surface.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Micro-agency on how Lucy carries shame and whether she drags or shields Ethel.

beat_map.moment: The Forelady inspects the immaculate belt and praises them. Then: "Speed it up a little!"
beat_map.dramatic_function: Beat ending type: ESCALATION. Praise becomes a worse threat.

beat_map.moment: Transition to evening. Brownstone exterior. Then the apartment living room, quiet, kitchen door closed.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Primary arc appears survived. New mystery enters through absence and the shut door.

beat_map.moment: Lucy and Ethel call for Ricky and Fred. No answer. Ethel asks for bicarbonate of soda and heads toward the kitchen.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Expose whether the player approaches the reveal with sympathy, suspicion, or scorekeeping.

beat_map.moment: The closed kitchen door becomes the session-end fulcrum.
beat_map.dramatic_function: Branching Choice 4. Session-end hook on mutual_grace_vs_scorekeeping.

pressure_points.source_moment: The Forelady's opening warning and the first shudder of the conveyor belt.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Threat and performance pressure. The player wants to show nerve, bluff, or caution before any competence is proven.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Small reactions like leaning in, deflecting, or going stiff let the player define Lucy's posture toward authority while the belt still starts on schedule.

pressure_points.source_moment: The first near-miss at the packing-room opening when the belt begins outrunning their hands.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Practical panic and partner pressure. The player feels the machine winning and Ethel becoming part of the problem or part of the bond.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Observation, softening, or intensifying choices let the player react to Ethel and the system without changing the core fact that the backlog is coming.

pressure_points.source_moment: The apartment kitchen disaster: ceiling chickens, sink scrubbing, and the rice flood.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Comic superiority and absurd relief. The player wants to laugh, gloat, or register grim recognition that the men are failing too.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Joking or dry observation gives texture to the mirrored failure while the sequence still lands as one concentrated reward beat.

pressure_points.source_moment: The concealment scramble before the Forelady's inspection and the later approach to the closed kitchen door.
pressure_points.emotional_pressure: Humiliation, survival, and then scorekeeping temptation. The player feels both bodily embarrassment and the urge to decide how hard to come down on Ricky afterward.
pressure_points.micro_agency_without_diverting_spine: Withdrawing, pushing, or hardening choices shape how Lucy carries disgrace and approaches the next confrontation, but do not interrupt the inspection or the end-hook reveal setup.

next_session_awareness.seed_for_next_session: The session must plant the closed kitchen door as proof that the men have also failed, and frame Lucy's final approach as a choice between grace and scorekeeping before the mutual surrender scene.

branching_choices.source_moment: The Forelady gives the last warning in the wrapping department, the belt starts, and the first easy chocolates arrive before the first speed jump.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The belt shudders and starts. One piece comes. Then the next. Slow enough to flatter you, which is dangerous. Ethel is beside you, rumpled from three other failures, and the wall opening at the far end might as well be a firing squad with trim.
branching_choices.choice_question: When the first candy threatens to set the tone, how do you take your place on the line?

options.label: A
options.text: You keep your eyes on the belt and save your talk for when you know what it can do.
options.outcome: You do not waste breath on bravery. Your hands move first. Pick up, wrap, set down. Pick up, wrap, set down. The papers rasp under your fingers. The belt hums. Ethel glances over once, waiting for a joke, and gets none. That steadies her more than a speech would. The Forelady, halfway to the door, gives you one last look and finds nothing to correct. Good. For ten seconds the room behaves. Then a voice through the wall barks, "Speed it up!" and the whole little arrangement changes shape at once. The spacing tightens. One piece bumps the next. Your quiet has bought you a clear look at the problem. It has not bought you time. Nothing has.
options.downstream_effect: You enter the first acceleration with a cleaner read on the machinery, but less social momentum to rally Ethel.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy answers pressure with observation instead of bluff.
promises_made: Lucy silently commits to reading the line before boasting.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Forelady leaves without another word. Ethel works a little closer to your rhythm.
options.defining_line: You do not perform readiness. You count inches.

options.label: B
options.text: You grin at the threat and attack the first run like speed can be bullied.
options.outcome: You meet the belt the way you meet most bad ideas — chin up and already in motion. Pick up, wrap, set down. Faster. Faster than necessary, which is another way of saying showy. Ethel catches it and hurries to match you. For a breath, it works. The papers fly. Your fingers snap. The Forelady pauses in the doorway, not approving exactly, but not stopping you either. Then the wall voice comes. "Speed it up!" The belt jumps as if insulted. Your early flourish turns on you at once. You have set the pace high, and now the machine raises it higher. One chocolate skids crooked through your hands. Another bumps your wrist. Confidence stays on your face. The trouble does not.
options.downstream_effect: Your aggressive start raises the room's pressure and pulls Ethel into your pace before the true backlog begins.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust -1 for being dragged into your tempo
suspicion +1 for visible levity under pressure

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy turns correction into performance.
key_successes_failures: Early wrapping rhythm built on bluffing speed.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's hands start chasing yours instead of the belt. The Forelady's eyes stay on your station a beat too long.
options.defining_line: You try to beat the machine before it has introduced itself.

options.label: C
options.text: You work carefully and keep each piece neat, even if it means staying by the rule instead of the pace.
options.outcome: You take the job at its own word. One piece. One paper. Fold clean. Set it back square. Ethel sees you doing it properly and copies the shape of your hands. The belt looks manageable because, at this speed, it is. The trick is that this speed is a lie. The Forelady watches long enough to see two obedient workers and moves off satisfied. Good. The room gives you exactly one small reward for doing it right. Then the voice from the other room cuts in. "Speed it up!" The belt jerks faster. Your neat corners become luxuries. A wrapped piece leaves your hand half a second late. Another arrives. Then another. The rule is still the rule. The time for it has already gone.
options.downstream_effect: You establish a standard of proper work that makes the coming failure feel sharper and more public.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1 for giving her a clear method

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Lucy commits to doing the work by the stated rule.
defining_moral_choices: Lucy chooses order over flourish at the first test.

options.world_noticed_signal: The papers at your station stay flat and square for a little while. The belt takes that personally.
options.defining_line: You give the rule a fair chance. The belt does not.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The first speed increase and near-miss at the packing-room opening.

canon_boundaries: The conveyor belt starts at manageable speed and can be increased from another room.
If unwrapped candy reaches the packing room, firing risk is real.
The Forelady values output and obedience, not personality.
No supernatural rescue, sabotage, or impossible mastery is allowed.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: If you go bold, she follows with weaker conviction. If you go practical, she settles. If you turn cold, she gets nervous but stays loyal.
She cuts off jokes, ignores charm, and rewards only visible competence or compliance.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy and Ethel know the rule and can see the wall opening, but they do not yet know the next speed jump timing. The Forelady does not know future player intent, only visible conduct.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Immediate pressure, public evaluation, and the need to establish how Lucy behaves under a machine's timetable.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: All paths fold into the same first acceleration and backlog threat.
fold_back_path.why: The scene's canon requires the belt to outrun ordinary wrapping and force a more consequential methodology choice.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Any freeform response that bluffs, jokes at authority, overreaches, or tries to dominate the pace.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Any freeform response that follows instructions, wraps carefully, or treats the task with procedural respect.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Any freeform response that observes, withholds performance, or conserves effort until the system reveals itself.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: How do you meet pressure before failure has earned its first laugh.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Echoes S2:E19 persistence_vs_self_protection payoff by determining whether Lucy enters the crisis as rattled, showy, or methodical.

branching_choices.source_moment: The production-room voice shouts, "Speed it up!" One piece nearly gets through unwrapped, backlog forms, and Ethel's rhythm starts to slip.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The easy part is over. One chocolate nearly slips past your hands and into the next room. Ethel grabs your arm. Behind the piece in your fingers, two more arrive, then three. The wall opening is no longer part of the room. It is a clock.
branching_choices.choice_question: As the line starts beating both of you, how do you handle Ethel?

options.label: A
options.text: You slow just enough to steady Ethel and keep the two of you working as one line instead of two panics.
options.outcome: You spend a second you cannot spare. That is the whole choice. "Here," you snap, not mean, just fast, and show Ethel the fold again with your own hands still moving. Pick up, wrap, set down. She catches it. For three beats you are not two frightened women. You are one bad little machine with four hands. The belt still wins ground. Of course it does. But it does not split you. A crooked piece comes your way and Ethel snatches it before the wall opening can have it. Her cap slips. She jams it back on. No speech. No gratitude. Just work. When the next surge comes, you are both still in trouble. The difference is that you are in the same trouble.
options.downstream_effect: Ethel becomes a more reliable partner in the next scramble, even as the backlog continues to grow.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +2 for active protection under pressure

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Lucy steadies Ethel instead of abandoning her.
sacrifices_made: Lucy gives up speed to preserve the partnership.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel stops chasing your hands and starts matching them. The pile still grows, but it grows in order.
options.defining_line: You spend time like money and give it to Ethel.

options.label: B
options.text: You focus on clearing your own station and trust Ethel to save herself if she can.
options.outcome: You make the practical cut. One station is a problem. Two stations are a funeral. So you keep your eyes on your own stretch of belt and work it hard. Pick up, wrap, set down. No coaching. No blame either. Just triage. Ethel sees it and understands too much too fast. Her mouth tightens. She works faster, sloppier. A piece bumps past her wrist and you do not go for it because yours are already stacking. She catches it herself at the last inch. Good. That is what you needed. The room narrows. The belt is now your belt, her belt, and the hole in the wall waiting for either one of you to fail. You stay afloat a little longer. Together is not the word for it.
options.downstream_effect: Your station stays briefly cleaner, but Ethel's strain increases and the duo starts to fray.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy chooses survival math over shared risk.
promises_kept_or_broken: Lucy does not fully back her partner under pressure.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel quits looking to you for cues. The space between your stations feels wider than the belt.
options.defining_line: You save your corner first and call it sense.

options.label: C
options.text: You bark at Ethel to move faster and turn the panic into a race she has to keep up with.
options.outcome: You do not comfort. You light a fire. "Come on," you shoot at Ethel, and your own hands answer first. Faster. Faster. The papers crumple under the speed. Wrapped enough becomes good enough because the wall opening is coming and that is the only judge that matters. Ethel startles, then lunges to keep up. It works in the worst possible way. The two of you clear a burst of candy with wild, ugly efficiency. Corners stick up. One wrapper tears. Another gets slapped flat by your palm and sent on its way. The line looks better for a breath. Ethel does not. Her nerves are now part of the machinery, jerking and snapping in time with yours. You have made motion. You have not made safety.
options.downstream_effect: The backlog clears in rough bursts, but Ethel becomes more anxious and the room's margin for error shrinks.

npc_dispositions.shift: suspicion +1 if rough output is noticed later

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Lucy drives Ethel harder under threat.
defining_moral_choices: Lucy converts fear into force.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's hands start jerking instead of folding. The wrappers on the belt look hurried because they are.
options.defining_line: You turn partnership into pace.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Lucy realizes the belt cannot be beaten honestly and begins looking for an illegal way to survive.

canon_boundaries: The line must visibly outrun human hands at this speed.
Ethel remains loyal but more nervous and practical than Lucy.
The Forelady is offstage for this stretch and reacts later to visible output, not private intentions.
No option can solve the wrapping crisis through sudden competence.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: If protected, she steadies and follows. If ignored, she grows short and worried. If pushed, she obeys with fear showing.
She will only care later whether the belt is clear, the wrappers passable, and discipline intact.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy and Ethel can see the backlog and know firing risk. They do not know when the Forelady will re-enter. No one yet knows the concealment tactic will be used.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Shared labor is turning into public failure, and the player must define whether partnership is a shield, a burden, or a tool.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: All paths fold into the same realization that legal wrapping cannot keep pace.
fold_back_path.why: This choice changes relationship texture and later cooperation, not the fact of mechanical defeat.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Any freeform response that orders, pressures, shames, or accelerates Ethel into frantic motion.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Any freeform response that teaches, coordinates, shares burden, or keeps orderly teamwork under threat.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Any freeform response that narrows focus to self-preservation without openly betraying Ethel.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: Do you hold the pair together, cut yourself loose, or turn panic into propulsion.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch dimension reference: session 3 event 24, solidarity_vs_self_preservation, paid off in Forelady inspection tone and Ethel loyalty during concealment.

branching_choices.source_moment: Chocolates pile up faster than hands can clear them, the packing-room opening is close, and Lucy sees no legal way to keep every piece from going through unwrapped.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The belt has stopped pretending. Chocolates stack at your station in a soft brown traffic jam. The wall opening waits. Ethel is already looking at you for the next bad idea. There are only three kinds left: the honest one, the practical one, and the one you will have to wear home on your body.
branching_choices.choice_question: When there is no clean way out, what do you do with the candy?

options.label: A
options.text: You hide the chocolates in your mouth, cap, and smock and pull Ethel into the same cheat.
options.outcome: You choose survival with both hands. One piece goes into your mouth. Then two. Your cheeks round out at once. Ethel sees it and freezes for half a beat. "Do it," you tell her, because there is no time for democracy. The belt keeps coming. Your cap lifts. Candy under it. Cap back down. Lumpy now. Your smock takes the next load. Pockets. Front fold. Anywhere cloth will lie and not confess. Ethel follows, badly but faithfully. Piece by piece, the two of you stop looking like workers and start looking upholstered. The belt behind you clears. The Forelady returns. Inspects. Sees only order. "Fine. You're doing splendidly." Then she leans to the wall and calls, "Speed it up a little!" Nobody moves. There is nowhere left.
options.downstream_effect: The line passes inspection, but your bodies become the hiding place and the next pressure jump becomes unbearable.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: factory_uniform_and_cap concealment_status set to used_to_hide_candy; chocolates_on_line concealed_status set to hidden_on_body

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1 if previously protected, or trust -1 if dragged hard into the cheat
trust +1 for apparent output, suspicion latent

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed: Lucy hides factory chocolates in mouth, cap, and uniform.
key_successes_failures: The wrapping line is survived only through concealment.
npcs_helped_or_harmed: Lucy pulls Ethel into a rule-breaking survival tactic.

options.world_noticed_signal: The belt runs clean for the first time in minutes. Your cap no longer fits the shape of your head.
options.defining_line: You make your own body part of the equipment.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the unwrapped candy go and take the rule as written rather than cheat your way past it.
options.outcome: You stop lying to the room. That is what this amounts to. Your hands keep moving, but when the next cluster comes you do not stuff it into your hat or your mouth or any other place dignity would hate. One piece rides past half wrapped. Then another, naked as a mistake, slips through the wall opening. Ethel makes a small sound and reaches after it too late. The belt keeps going because machinery has no use for principle. The Forelady comes in on the evidence. One look at the opening. One look at the wrappers piling crooked on the belt. Her face does not change. Worse. She stops the line with a call sharp enough to cut paper. You stand there with chocolate on your fingers and nothing hidden anywhere except maybe pride. Even that is not in great shape.
options.downstream_effect: The failure becomes visible at once, inviting direct Forelady intervention instead of secret survival.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1 for not dragging her into cheating

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy accepts visible failure rather than cheat.
sacrifices_made: Lucy preserves the rule at the cost of job security.
key_successes_failures: Unwrapped candy reaches the packing room.

options.world_noticed_signal: A bare piece vanishes through the wall opening. The room feels suddenly much smaller.
options.defining_line: You let the rule catch you in the open.

options.label: C
options.text: You keep wrapping as long as you can and knock the worst pieces aside to buy seconds without fully committing to the cheat.
options.outcome: You try the middle road, which in a factory is just another edge. Your hands keep wrapping the pieces you can save cleanly. The rest you bat aside from the opening, stacking them off the live path in a little brown drift at your station. It buys seconds. Real seconds. Ethel catches on and starts doing the same, one hand wrapping, one hand shoving trouble out of sight. The belt looks better from a distance and worse up close. Chocolates gather against the wood lip, under the paper stack, near your elbow. A hidden mess. Not a solved one. When the Forelady returns, the opening is clear enough to pass first glance. Then her eye drops to the side pile. Not much. Enough. Her mouth tightens. She has seen workers improvise before. She does not admire it.
options.downstream_effect: You delay open failure and avoid bodily concealment, but create a visible side mess that sharpens Forelady scrutiny.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust 0 to +1 for shared practical workaround

emotional_ledger_entries: crimes_committed_or_witnessed: Lucy conceals workflow failure by diverting product off the line.
defining_moral_choices: Lucy chooses a partial workaround instead of full obedience or full cheat.
key_successes_failures: The line is kept clear only by creating a side backlog.

options.world_noticed_signal: The wall opening looks respectable. Your station does not.
options.defining_line: You move the problem sideways and call that time.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The Forelady's inspection and the evening transition back to the apartment, with the day's failure now fixed in body, memory, or authority pressure.

canon_boundaries: The wrapping crisis cannot be solved by sudden mastery.
The Forelady's inspection must matter and produce visible judgment.
The world remains ordinary factory reality with material consequences.
Humor must stay prop-based and bodily, not meta or verbal-only.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: She will follow a cheat if Lucy moves first, but fear shows. She will also respect not being dragged into one.
She responds to output, evidence, and discipline. Praise is brief and only for apparent efficiency.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy knows the rule is impossible at current speed. She does not know exactly when the Forelady returns, but knows inspection is likely. Ethel follows Lucy's lead more than her own invention.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Humiliation, labor panic, and the choice between dignity, survival, and compromise under institutional pressure.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: All paths lead to evening survival of the workday and carry different residue into the apartment return.
fold_back_path.why: The session's primary arc is surviving the day, but how survival is achieved changes shame, solidarity, and the irony of later chocolate gifts.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Any freeform response that hides candy on the body, eats it, breaks protocol openly, or escalates the workaround.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Any freeform response that accepts visible failure, calls for the line to stop, or refuses concealment on principle.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Any freeform response that diverts, delays, side-stacks, or half-conceals without fully embracing either rule or cheat.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: No clean answer. Only what kind of wrong you can live with.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch dimension reference: session 3 event 26, rule_following_vs_improvised_cheating, paid off by the ironic weight of the five_pound_chocolate_boxes and final surrender tone.

branching_choices.source_moment: Lucy and Ethel return to the quiet apartment. Ricky and Fred are absent. Ethel asks for bicarbonate of soda and heads toward the closed kitchen door.
branching_choices.narrative_setup: The apartment is too quiet. The laundry basket is still on the sofa. The dust rag is still on the radio. The kitchen door is closed, which is new, and therefore guilty. Ethel puts a hand to her stomach and heads for it. You are one step behind her, tired enough to forgive anything and sore enough not to.
branching_choices.choice_question: At the closed kitchen door, how do you take the next step?

options.label: A
options.text: You stop Ethel at the door and decide to hear Ricky out before you give the room its verdict.
options.outcome: You put a hand out and catch Ethel by the sleeve before the knob turns. Not kindness exactly. Caution. A shut kitchen door, after a day like this, is either a warning or an alibi. "Wait," you say, and call into the apartment first. "Ricky?" Nothing. Then again, louder. The quiet holds. Ethel looks at you, one hand still on her stomach, one on the knob. Between the living room and that door, the whole wager seems to be standing on tiptoe. If Ricky answers now, he gets a chance to frame the wreck before you see it. If he does not, the silence frames it for him. Either way, you have chosen terms. The room can wait one more second. Barely.
options.downstream_effect: The reveal is delayed and the next session opens on whether Ricky answers, stays hidden, or lets the kitchen speak first.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: kitchen_door_note visibility may be noticed before door opens

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1 for being granted a chance to explain

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Lucy grants room for explanation before accusation.
secrets_discovered: Lucy recognizes the closed door as evidence before proof.

options.world_noticed_signal: The apartment gives you nothing back. Even the kitchen door seems to hold its breath.
options.defining_line: You make silence testify first.
options.next_session_opens: The next session opens on your hand still raised, the kitchen door shut, and Ricky's answer either coming too late or not at all.

options.label: B
options.text: You let Ethel open the door carefully and take whatever is there as shared proof that everybody lost.
options.outcome: You do not rush her and you do not stop her. Ethel has asked for bicarbonate of soda, not revenge, and that sets the tone better than anything else in the room. She reaches for the knob. You stay close. Not braced for battle. Braced for fact. The living room around you is already evidence enough — the tilted ironing board, the dust rag, the laundry basket still waiting for a better class of husband. Whatever is behind that door belongs to all four of you now. If it is a disaster, it is part of the same long joke the factory has already played on your cap, your smock, your feet. The knob turns under Ethel's hand. You are ready to see it. Not to win with it.
options.downstream_effect: The next session opens on the kitchen reveal landing as mutual evidence, priming a softer route toward surrender.

persistent_state_changes.inventory: kitchen_door_note may be read and obeyed only insofar as it confirms the mess

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +2 for approaching the reveal without scorekeeping

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: Lucy gives up the chance to weaponize the reveal.
defining_moral_choices: Lucy approaches failure as shared proof, not leverage.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's hand stays steady on the knob. The room behind you looks tired instead of angry.
options.defining_line: You choose evidence over triumph.
options.next_session_opens: The next session opens on the kitchen door swinging inward and the full domestic wreck standing there like a signed confession.

options.label: C
options.text: You push past Ethel and throw the door open at once before anybody can explain or soften it.
options.outcome: You have had enough of warnings, doors, rules, belts, and men who are probably somewhere not answering. So you move. Past Ethel. Hand on knob. Door open now. If there is a note, it gets no vote. If Ricky has an explanation, he can bring it into the smell. The whole day has been other people telling you how much room there is for error. Fine. Here is your turn. Ethel lets out your name, half protest, half relief, because somebody was going to do it and of course it was you. The door starts back on its hinges. Whatever is in that kitchen is about to come into the living room all at once — the mess, the proof, the score, the laugh, all of it. You have decided there will be no soft opening.
options.downstream_effect: The next session opens on an immediate hard reveal, with Ricky and Fred denied any chance to prepare the ground.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust +1 for decisive action or -1 if she feels overrun

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: Lucy forces the hidden kitchen truth into the open.
defining_moral_choices: Lucy chooses confrontation over grace.

options.world_noticed_signal: The knob turns hard in your hand. Ethel's shoes stop short behind you.
options.defining_line: You do not knock on your own evidence.
options.next_session_opens: The next session opens with the kitchen smell hitting first, then the wreck, then whatever Ricky thinks can still be called an explanation.

branching_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The kitchen reveal and the reunion with Ricky and Fred in the next session's opening beat.

canon_boundaries: The apartment is real, walkable space with a genuinely closed kitchen door hiding domestic disaster.
The men have failed visibly at housework and cooking.
The bet must end without a decisive winner, though the tone of surrender can vary.
The session must end on the player's unresolved decision, not on a completed reveal.

character_truth.authentic_reaction_to_unexpected_input: She follows Lucy's lead but voices practical concern. She is more likely to hesitate than charge.
If granted space, he tries to explain and reset terms. If ambushed, he gets defensive before softening.
He covers embarrassment with deadpan nonsense and broad pronouncements.

scene_integrity.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy and Ethel know only that the apartment is quiet and the kitchen is closed. They do not yet know the exact state of the kitchen or where Ricky and Fred are standing in relation to it.
scene_integrity.emotional_context: Exhaustion after public failure, temptation to gloat, and the first chance to decide whether the day ends as a contest or a draw.

fold_back_path.nearest_safe_outcome: All paths fold into the next session's kitchen reveal and reunion scene.
fold_back_path.why: The unresolved decision is the retention hook, and the reveal itself belongs just beyond this session.

freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_chaotic: Any freeform response that opens the door immediately, ignores warnings, or forces the reveal into the room.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_lawful: Any freeform response that lets the reveal happen carefully, shares it with Ethel, or approaches it as common evidence.
freeform_alignment_mapping.maps_to_neutral: Any freeform response that delays action to gather information, call for Ricky, or control the terms before opening.
freeform_alignment_mapping.spirit_at_this_moment: Will you meet the hidden mess with caution, grace, or a hard swing.

branching_choices.cross_session_payoff_reference: Branch dimension reference: session 3 event 30, mutual_grace_vs_scorekeeping, paid off in next-session reunion tone and final bet outcome.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The Forelady has just given the last-chance warning and Lucy tries to look ready beside Ethel.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The warning lands and stays in the air. The belt has not started yet. That is almost worse.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What do you let show on your face before the first piece arrives?

options.label: A
options.text: You wear a bright little smile as if the rule just challenged the wrong woman.
options.outcome: The smile goes on a shade too fast. That is part of its charm and all of its danger. Ethel sees it and tries one of her own. Hers looks rented. The Forelady does not return it. She just takes you in, cap to shoes, and leaves the room with the expression of a woman who has seen confidence before and usually had to mop after it. The belt starts under that look. Your grin stays up another second. Then your hands have to earn it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration sharpens around posture, bluff, and the gap between expression and competence.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy answers threat with visible bravado.

options.world_noticed_signal: The Forelady's eyes rest on your smile and move on unimpressed.

options.label: B
options.text: You give the warning a plain nod and save your energy for the work.
options.outcome: You do not decorate the moment. A nod. That's all. The kind that says you heard every word and do not intend to waste one back. Ethel relaxes a hair. The Forelady likes that better than charm, or at least dislikes it less. The room stays practical for one clean breath. Belt. Paper. Wall opening. Rule. When the chocolates begin to come, they arrive in a room you have not made any harder with performance.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows steadier and more procedural, with less emphasis on bluff.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Lucy meets the warning with plain acceptance.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's shoulders drop a fraction. The room stays all business.

options.label: C
options.text: You keep your face blank and watch the belt instead of the woman.
options.outcome: You give the Forelady nothing to work with. No grin. No flinch. Just your eyes dropping to the belt, the papers, the distance to the wall opening. Ethel notices and follows your look. Good. There is more use in the machinery than in the warning. The Forelady leaves with no fresh reason to remember you. That helps. A little. The belt starts, and the first chocolates come through into a silence you have kept clear on purpose.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into observation, spacing, and practical threat surfaces.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy withholds performance and studies the system.

options.world_noticed_signal: The belt gets your attention before the Forelady can keep it.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The first chocolates appear and the wrapping rhythm begins.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Ethel arrives rumpled from multiple departments and admits she has already been kicked out of several places in the factory.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: Ethel looks like the factory has been mailing her back and forth all day. Her cap is crooked. Her nerves are not far behind it.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you answer Ethel when she admits this is her fourth department?

options.label: A
options.text: You tell her the two of you will get through this one together.
options.outcome: You answer her the way a pal answers. Fast and plain. No teasing. No grand speech. Just enough to make the next ten seconds bearable. Ethel looks at you and believes half of it, which is good for Ethel. The other half she borrows from your face. That is good for both of you. The belt has not sped up yet. There is still room for companionship before the machinery starts charging rent on it.
options.tonal_effect: Narration warms around paired action and shared burden.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Lucy reassures Ethel before the pressure spike.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's mouth stops pinching at the corners.

options.label: B
options.text: You tell her you did not do so well either and leave it at that.
options.outcome: You meet her halfway. Not comfort exactly. Not distance either. Just a fact laid beside her fact. You did not do so well either. That is honest enough to keep her from feeling singled out, and spare enough not to spend heart you may need later. Ethel nods. The two of you stand there like women comparing bruises under winter coats. Then the belt asks for hands instead of sympathy.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays dry, practical, and companionable without softening much.

emotional_ledger_entries: promises_made: Lucy shares failure plainly with Ethel.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel gives one quick nod and gets back to the belt.

options.label: C
options.text: You joke that at this rate she will see every department before supper.
options.outcome: You throw her a joke because that is the tool nearest your hand. It lands. Barely. Ethel gives a short laugh that sounds like she found it on the floor. Still, a laugh is a laugh, and the room could use one before the machine starts making decisions for everybody. The trouble with joke logic is that it has to clear the room fast or it turns into evidence. The belt starts before the line can go stale.
options.tonal_effect: Narration tilts toward bluff and bright reaction masking strain.

npc_dispositions.shift: trust 0 to +1 for trying to lighten it

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy answers embarrassment with a joke.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel laughs once and then looks right back at the belt.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The women begin wrapping in temporary rhythm.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The first near-miss at the packing-room opening when one chocolate almost gets through unwrapped.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: One piece almost gets away from you. That is enough to change the room. The wall opening has a mouth now.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What do you say in the first real burst of panic?

options.label: A
options.text: You say very little and start counting the danger instead of naming it.
options.outcome: You do not waste the breath on alarm. Your eyes do the math instead. One piece in hand. Two behind it. Three if Ethel misses hers. The opening in the wall is now less than an arm's reach from disaster. Ethel hears nothing from you but the paper crackle and takes that as a cue to move, not freeze. It is not calming. It is useful. In this room, useful wins by a nose.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes tighter, more measured, and more mechanical.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy restrains panic into calculation.

options.world_noticed_signal: The papers snap faster in your hands than your voice ever could.

options.label: B
options.text: You let the panic out loud and try to outrun it with noise.
options.outcome: The words come out ahead of the plan. Fast, bright, and no help at all except that they keep you from choking on the pressure. Ethel startles, then moves faster because there is no other place for the noise to go. For one burst, the two of you work on pure nerves. It is ugly and alive. The belt appreciates neither quality, but it does keep moving while you do.
options.tonal_effect: Narration grows sharper and more embarrassed, with clipped reaction beats.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy externalizes pressure instead of containing it.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's head snaps toward you, then back to the belt.

options.label: C
options.text: You tell Ethel to keep going and make your voice sound steadier than the room is.
options.outcome: You put a lid on it and hand Ethel the version she can use. Keep going. That's all. A small sentence. A working sentence. She takes it and does exactly that. The panic does not leave. It just moves into the fingers where it belongs. The wrappers get rougher. The pace gets worse. But the room does not split open around the fear, and that counts for something. Not much. Enough.
options.tonal_effect: Narration steadies and treats panic as something to be managed through action.

emotional_ledger_entries: npcs_helped_or_harmed: Lucy contains panic to steady Ethel.

options.world_noticed_signal: Ethel's hands stop fluttering and start folding again.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Lucy concludes the line is beating them and looks for a workaround.

emotional_choices.source_moment: Fred shows off the broom-newspaper invention and reveals the collapsed seven-layer cake.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The apartment has slipped. Then Fred walks in with a broom, a sports section, and the confidence of a man about to be corrected by a cake.
emotional_choices.choice_question: How do you take the sight of Fred's invention and that flat dessert?

options.label: A
options.text: You enjoy it openly because somebody else's confidence has finally gone soft first.
options.outcome: The laugh comes easy here. Easier than anything in the factory. Fred with his newspaper broom. Ricky trying to be polite to a cake that looks like it lost a fight with gravity. The room deserves every bit of your amusement. The men are still talking as if this counts as know-how. That helps. The broader they play confidence, the flatter the cake sits. Good. The joke has done its own wrapping.
options.tonal_effect: Narration leans into status undercut and blunt comic coda.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: The men's domestic confidence is visibly fraudulent.

options.world_noticed_signal: The cake sits there, one inch tall and entirely sincere.

options.label: B
options.text: You take it in dryly and let the objects do the talking.
options.outcome: You do not need to help the joke. The broom already has a newspaper clipped to it. The cake already looks like breakfast pretending to be dessert. Ricky and Fred stand beside both objects with the last of their confidence still neatly pressed. That is enough. The room reports itself. Nicely. Then the kitchen starts asking for a bigger laugh.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays deadpan and lets props carry the comic weight.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: The men's dinner plan is already visibly compromised.

options.world_noticed_signal: Nobody has to say what happened to the other six layers. The plate has already testified.

options.label: C
options.text: You feel a little sympathy because the room already looks one bad minute from worse.
options.outcome: The laugh still lands. It just lands with a little mercy under it. The cake is absurd. Fred is proud of nonsense. Ricky is trying to hold the whole thing upright with politeness. But the kitchen doorway behind them is already untidy in a way that promises labor, not victory. You can see the next trouble setting the table. That softens the laugh without spoiling it. Not much. Just enough to make the men look less smug and more doomed.
options.tonal_effect: Narration keeps the comedy but shades it toward shared overreach.

emotional_ledger_entries: sacrifices_made: Lucy withholds full mockery at the first sign of the men's collapse.

options.world_noticed_signal: The joke stays funny. The kitchen keeps hissing anyway.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: Ricky and Fred move into the kitchen to confront rice math and the pressure cooker.

emotional_choices.source_moment: The pressure cooker explodes, the chickens are found on the ceiling, and the men wash them in the sink while rice begins to rise.
emotional_choices.narrative_lead_in: The pressure cooker goes off like a gunshot. Then there are chickens on the ceiling. The room has moved past advice.
emotional_choices.choice_question: What kind of disbelief colors the sight of the men washing ceiling chickens in the sink?

options.label: A
options.text: You take it as proof that nobody in this bet knows what they are doing.
options.outcome: The disbelief comes with a verdict, but a fair one. Not on them alone. On the whole idea. Two men in undershirts are scrubbing chickens with dish soap as if this might still count as dinner. The sight is ridiculous enough to be honest. Housework has beaten the brag out of them. The kitchen is about to finish the job. There is comfort in that, even while the rice lid starts to lift in the background like another hand asking to be recognized.
options.tonal_effect: Narration frames the absurdity as shared evidence of mutual failure.

emotional_ledger_entries: defining_moral_choices: Lucy reads the men's disaster as common proof, not enemy weakness.

options.world_noticed_signal: The chickens drip in the sink. The rice lid rises behind them like it has its own opinion.

options.label: B
options.text: You savor the lunacy because the men have finally caught up to your kind of trouble.
options.outcome: This is the reward. Not the chickens alone. The speed of the fall. One second swagger, next second poultry on the ceiling and two grown men washing it off like a household custom. The room keeps topping itself. Good. Before the laughter can settle, the rice starts climbing out of its pot and the whole kitchen gets promoted from mistake to event. Nobody in here is in charge anymore. Best part of all, they still think they might be.
options.tonal_effect: Narration becomes broader, faster, and crueler only in the comic sense.

emotional_ledger_entries: secrets_discovered: The men are failing as spectacularly as the women.

options.world_noticed_signal: The men scrub harder as if soap could restore authority.

options.label: C
options.text: You stare at it dryly because the room has become too foolish to improve with commentary.
options.outcome: There are moments when a room outruns language. This is one. Chickens in the sink. Chicken marks on the ceiling. Fred handing over the brush like this is all listed in a cookbook somewhere. Ricky accepting it. The rice pot starting to bulge behind them. The scene has built its own punchline and is now busy adding side dishes. All you can do is watch it keep getting worse in orderly stages. Which it does.
options.tonal_effect: Narration stays flat, visual, and prop-driven, with the joke carried by sequence.

emotional_ledger_entries: key_successes_failures: The kitchen has crossed from strain into full shambles.

options.world_noticed_signal: The brush changes hands. The kitchen gets worse without hurrying.

emotional_choices.all_paths_arrive_at: The rice boils over and the kitchen becomes a white tide.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — the Forelady's warning lands and the belt has not yet started.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The warning hits the room and waits to see what it did to your shoulders.

options.label: A
options.text: You square up like she picked a fight.
options.narration_adjustment: Your chin comes up before the first chocolate does. The pose is good. The room is not impressed yet.

options.label: B
options.text: You smooth your apron and get small.
options.narration_adjustment: Your hands go to the apron as if neatness might count for skill. The Forelady notices order when she can get it.

options.label: C
options.text: You look past her to the belt.
options.narration_adjustment: You give the machinery your attention, not the speech. Good instinct. The belt is the one with the real authority here.

posture_shifts.placement: Early Setup — first chocolates arrive in easy rhythm.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: For a few seconds the job flatters you, and that is when most bad jobs do their best work.

options.label: A
options.text: You lean into it and move quick.
options.narration_adjustment: Your hands get ambitious before the room has earned it. The papers fly neatly enough. For now.

options.label: B
options.text: You keep the folds neat and exact.
options.narration_adjustment: Each wrapper gets the treatment of something that matters. The pace can still afford that. Barely.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — Ethel admits she has already failed through several departments.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Ethel's cap sits crooked on her head like the day put it there with a slap.

options.label: A
options.text: You edge a little closer to her station.
options.narration_adjustment: The move is small. Ethel sees it anyway. So does the space between your stations.

options.label: B
options.text: You stay put and save the energy.
options.narration_adjustment: You keep your ground and your breath. In this room, every inch and every second may need to be spent twice.

posture_shifts.placement: Mid Escalation — after "Speed it up!" and the first near-miss.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The belt jumps and your body decides something before your mouth does.

options.label: A
options.text: You hunch in and chase the pieces.
options.narration_adjustment: Your hands start grabbing at the work instead of meeting it. The chocolates notice. They keep coming.

options.label: B
options.text: You plant your feet and work from there.
options.narration_adjustment: Your shoes stay put even while the room speeds up around them. It gives your hands one honest thing to build from.

options.label: C
options.text: You shoot Ethel a look and dare the belt.
options.narration_adjustment: The look says this is now personal. Ethel catches it. The belt does not care, but it does seem amused.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Escalation — Lucy studies the spacing, wall opening, and impossible pace.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The trick is not in the candy. It is in the inches between your hands and that hole in the wall.

options.label: A
options.text: You clock the opening and count distance.
options.narration_adjustment: You stop seeing chocolates and start seeing trajectories. That is colder. Also smarter.

options.label: B
options.text: You refuse to look up from your hands.
options.narration_adjustment: You give all your faith to the next fold. It is not enough faith, but it is close at hand.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat — Fred reveals the broom-newspaper invention and the flat cake.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Fred stands there with complete confidence and an object that argues every word of it.

options.label: A
options.text: You enjoy the sight on principle.
options.narration_adjustment: The cake does not drape. It sits. Fred keeps talking anyway.

options.label: B
options.text: You take it in with a straight face.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not help the joke. The plate has already done the work.

posture_shifts.placement: During Breath beat — the rice starts climbing and the kitchen is about to go under.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The lid on the rice pot lifts a little, then a little more, like the room has one more secret and cannot hold it.

options.label: A
options.text: You wait for the room to finish embarrassing them.
options.narration_adjustment: You can see the disaster lining up its shoes. Ricky cannot. Not yet.

options.label: B
options.text: You tense up because you know that sound.
options.narration_adjustment: A kitchen gives warning before it gives ruin. The warning is in the lid. The ruin is right behind it.

posture_shifts.placement: During Twist beat — chocolates pile up and Lucy is seconds from choosing concealment or open failure.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: Your mouth, your cap, your pockets, the side of the station — the room starts offering bad storage ideas.

options.label: A
options.text: You go very still and think ugly thoughts.
options.narration_adjustment: The pause is tiny. It is enough to fit a whole bad plan inside it.

options.label: B
options.text: You move before dignity can object.
options.narration_adjustment: Once your hands commit, shame has to catch up later. It usually does.

posture_shifts.placement: Late Resolution — Lucy and Ethel stand in the quiet living room facing the closed kitchen door.
posture_shifts.narrator_line: The shut kitchen door sits in the apartment like a person who knows something and is waiting to be asked badly.

options.label: A
options.text: You come at it already keeping score.
options.narration_adjustment: After a day like yours, a hidden mess can look a lot like justice. The knob waits under that thought.

options.label: B
options.text: You come at it tired enough for mercy.
options.narration_adjustment: The day has wrung some fight out of you. What is left may still be sharp, but it is not eager.

options.label: C
options.text: You come at it like evidence.
options.narration_adjustment: You do not need the room to be worse. You only need it to be true.

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy and Ethel know the firing rule and can see the route to the packing room. They do not know how quickly the belt will accelerate. The Forelady knows only their visible competence, not their private intentions.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Cold pressure, public evaluation, and the need to establish Lucy's posture under threat fast.
canon_boundaries_active: Factory work is manual, timed, and physically demanding.
If unwrapped candy reaches the packing room, firing risk becomes real.
The Forelady values output and obedience over personality.
No sudden mastery or implausible rescue.
freeform_risk_areas: Trying to charm or debate the Forelady out of the rule
Inventing tools or machine controls not present in the room
Attempting to stop the line without consequences

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy and Ethel know the line is beating them and that inspection may return at any time. They do not know the precise timing of the Forelady's re-entry. The Forelady cannot see private exchanges while offstage.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Mechanical pressure, partner strain, and the shrinking space between embarrassment and firing.
canon_boundaries_active: The belt can speed up from another room.
Ethel remains more nervous and practical than Lucy.
Visible backlog and unwrapped candy matter immediately.
freeform_risk_areas: Player attempts to fully solve the line through skill alone
Player tries to leave the station or abandon Ethel entirely
Player claims the Forelady sees or hears offstage details she cannot

available_objects: broom with sports section clipped to handle
flat cake on plate under dish towel
scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Ricky and Fred know only their own kitchen trouble and do not know the women's exact factory ordeal yet. The player can observe the men's failure but cannot alter the established explosion and rice overflow sequence.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Reward beat through mirrored collapse, object-state escalation, and broad domestic absurdity.
canon_boundaries_active: Household tools and food behave materially.
The kitchen cannot stay neat if the pressure cooker and rice are mishandled this way.
Humor must remain visual, domestic, and consequence-light.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to prevent the explosion retroactively
Player introduces modern appliances or impossible cleanup
Player turns the scene into cruel ridicule rather than comic collapse

present_npcs.current_disposition: fearful but ready to follow Lucy
about to inspect, focused on output

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy knows there is no honest way to keep perfect pace. Ethel knows Lucy is choosing something drastic but depends on seeing it first. The Forelady knows only what inspection reveals.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Humiliation, bodily comedy, and a no-clean-answer choice between rule, workaround, and cheating.
canon_boundaries_active: The candy-factory line must become a pressure test survived through frantic concealment rather than true mastery.
The Forelady's praise, if earned, is brief and leads to more pressure.
No interior monologue explanation should replace visible action.
freeform_risk_areas: Player tries to talk the Forelady into mercy before evidence appears
Player invents hidden containers or exits not in scene
Player avoids all consequence through implausible dexterity

scene_rules_layer_4.character_knowledge_limits: Lucy and Ethel know the apartment is quiet and the kitchen is closed. They do not yet know the full kitchen state or where Ricky and Fred are. Ricky and Fred are absent from the room and cannot react until revealed or called.
scene_rules_layer_4.emotional_context: Exhaustion after labor, suspended judgment, and the temptation to turn the hidden room into leverage or mercy.
canon_boundaries_active: The women must return to an apartment that proves the men have also failed.
The bet must end with no decisive winner.
The session must end on the player's unresolved decision.
freeform_risk_areas: Player resolves the entire reunion inside this session
Player invents men already standing in the living room contradicting the setup
Player reframes the scene into explicit moral speech instead of prop-based suspense

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: At the first jump in belt speed, Ethel looks over for a cue, gets only your steady hands, and falls into your rhythm instead of speaking; the Forelady leaves the aisle without turning back.
paths.current_session_echo: Wrapping Line First Surge: when one crooked piece skids toward the wall opening, Ethel catches it cleanly without yelping because she is already working close to your pace.
paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment Kitchen Reveal: Ethel backs your read of the room with a low, practical line to Ricky — "She knew something was wrong the minute she saw that door."
paths.later_session_legacy: N/A
paths.defining_line_captured: You do not perform readiness. You count inches.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Ethel trust rises through practical mimicry, and Forelady suspicion drops into routine supervision.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ethel physically matches Lucy's hand rhythm and stops fishing for reassurance, while the Forelady exits the station without a corrective line.

paths.label: B

paths.current_session_echo: Wrapping Line First Surge: the first crooked piece clips your wrist because your early flourish has set the pace too high, and Ethel's wrapper tears trying to keep up.
paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment Reunion: if the kitchen reveal turns argumentative, Ethel snaps first with "I had enough racing for one day," before Ricky can frame the room.
paths.later_session_legacy: N/A

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Ethel trust drops through visible scrambling, and Forelady suspicion rises into close watch.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: Ethel mirrors Lucy too fast, fumbles a wrapper, and the Forelady lingers in the doorway instead of moving on.

resets: Doorway pause from the Forelady clears after inspection passes or fails.

key_successes_failures archive records bluff-built rhythm.

paths.current_session_echo: Wrapping Line First Surge: when the speed jumps, your neat corners start landing late, so the first visible failure is not sloppiness but lateness — one wrapped piece arrives half a second too late to feel safe.
paths.next_session_payoff: Apartment Kitchen Reveal: Ricky reads your face as tired rather than combative and opens with explanation instead of defense if given room — "Now look, we tried to do it right."

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Forelady trust rises through visible obedience, and Ethel gains confidence from a clear method.
consequence_visibility.how_player_sees_it: The wrappers sit square on the belt, Ethel copies the exact fold, and the Forelady leaves with no correction.

persists: Ethel remembers Lucy gave the rules a real try before cheating or failing.

paths.label: A

paths.current_session_echo: Illegal-Solution Realization: when the next surge comes, Ethel looks to you once, gets the cue, and starts matching the cheat or workaround you choose without freezing first.

paths.label: A
paths.immediate_effect: The belt suddenly runs clean, your cheeks fill out, your cap lifts off your head and comes back down lumpy, and the Forelady returns to find order where there was panic.
paths.current_session_echo: Forelady Inspection and Exit: she says, "Fine. You're doing splendidly," then leans to the wall and calls, "Speed it up a little!" while neither you nor Ethel can move because there is nowhere left to put another piece.

paths.defining_line_captured: You make your own body part of the equipment.

paths.next_session_payoff: Kitchen Reveal and Reunion: if Lucy grants Ricky room, he softens faster because she already took one loss in the open; he opens with "Now look, nobody did so hot today" instead of a defense speech.
paths.later_session_legacy: The five_pound_chocolate_boxes land less as insult and more as clumsy peace offering because Lucy did not survive the factory by cheating.
paths.defining_line_captured: You let the rule catch you in the open.

consequence_visibility.what_changes: Forelady trust drops into ready_to_fire, and failure becomes public instead of hidden.

paths.next_session_payoff: Kitchen Reveal and Peace Offering: the five_pound_chocolate_boxes land with a practical, bitter edge; Lucy eyes the box corners and the room at once, as if one more neat package is trying to pass inspection.

paths.defining_line_captured: You move the problem sideways and call that time.
